title: snap! only checks media size when saving projects
body: right now the cloud server has a max 10mb on the total request, but snap! only warns users when the _media_ is larger than 10mb. \r \r we need to either update the checking in the api library, or update the server itself, but it seems easier to update the cloud.js library.  the server won't be able to tell the media part from the non-media part with the way we're doing things.
text: snap! only checks media size when saving projects. right now the cloud server has a max 10mb on the total request, but snap! only warns users when the _media_ is larger than 10mb. \r \r we need to either update the checking in the api library, or update the server itself, but it seems easier to update the cloud.js library.  the server won't be able to tell the media part from the non-media part with the way we're doing things.

title: error on creating provider with incorrect ip address format
body: i made a typo entering value for  generic_ip_address  and  post /providers/generic  returns status code 500. \r \r here's the last lines of log:\r \r    pytb\r     return  0 <= int x  < 256 for x in re.split '\\.', re.match r'^\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+$', ip .group 0   .count true  == 4\r attributeerror: 'nonetype' object has no attribute 'group'\r    \r \r note, the inputted value itself  addr:10.10.10.1 .\r \r digging from  sourcecode  https://github.com/gluufederation/gluu-flask/blob/01c5ada932a32782d246329375135c9846b9d1c0/gluuapi/reqparser/provider.py l27 , as seen below:\r \r    python\r return  0 <= int x  < 256 for x in re.split '\\.', re.match r'^\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+$', ip .group 0   .count true  == 4\r    \r \r  re.match r'^\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+$', ip   will return  none  object since the regex is not matched, which as we know  none  doesn't have any  group  attribute/method.

title: spring context is not closed after a related test class is finished
body: i create two test classes:\r  @springboottest\r class spec1 extends specification {\r ...\r }\r  \r  @springboottest\r class spec2 extends specification {\r ...\r }\r  \r they are both run  e.g. using  mvn test  .\r assuming that the tests are run in order: spec1, spec2.\r a spring context is created for spec1 and another one for spec2.\r currently the spring context for spec1 is not closed before running spec2, the spring context for spec1 is destroyed together with spring context for spec2 after all tests are finished.\r current behavior causes that a bean  a  possessed by spec1 context is alive during spec2 execution and thus spec1 and spec2 tests are not really self contained.\r assume that you create a queue consumer bean. if the bean for spec1 is alive during spec2 execution, messages that are expected to be consumed by spec2 queue consumer could be consumed by spec1 queue consumer which imho is unexpected behavior.

title: configuring guzzle or monolog via \\feedio\\factory does not work
body: hi!,\r \r     versions affected\r i'm using newest stable   3.0.1   release of the library. it's irrelevant though, as the problem occurs in all previous versions.\r \r     background \r i'm using  \\feedio\\factory::create  to create standard  feedio  instance:\r    php\r $feedio = \\feedio\\factory::create \r          \r             'builder' => 'monolog',\r             'config' =>   ,\r          \r      ->getfeedio  ;\r    \r \r it works fine this way. the design of passing config indicates that you should be able to specify  monolog\\logger  or  guzzlehttp\\client  config under  config  key and  \\feedio\\factory  should pass the to specific builders, then to actual class. this does not happen.\r \r examples suggest this is the intended case for configuration:\r    php\r $feedio = \\feedio\\factory::create \r          \r             'builder' => 'monolog',\r            'config' =>  \r                 'test' => 'this should be passed to monolog logger constructor',\r              ,\r          ,\r          \r             'builder' => 'guzzleclient',\r             'config' =>  \r                 'test' => 'this should be passed to guzzle client constructor',\r              ,\r          \r      ->getfeedio  ;\r    \r \r     problem\r there are two problems.\r 1. monologbuilder. reflection api in  \\feedio\\factory::getbuilder  is used incorrectly.  newinstanceargs  takes an array an passes members one by one to the constructor. it does not pass the whole array to class constructor.  \\feedio\\factory\\builder\\monologbuilder::__construct  is prepared to inject an array. to pull this off, this is how you'd have to craft your config:\r    php\r $feedio = \\feedio\\factory::create \r          \r             'builder' => 'monolog',\r             'config' =>  \r                 'config' =>  \r                     'test' => true,\r                  \r              ,\r          \r      ->getfeedio  ;\r    \r \r 2. guzzleclientbuilder. it is not ready to be constructed with params  there is no constructor to capture the config and pass it to guzzle client . if you do:\r    php\r $feedio = \\feedio\\factory::create \r          \r             'builder' => 'monolog',\r             'config' =>  \r              ,\r          ,\r          \r             'builder' => 'guzzleclient',\r             'config' =>  \r                 'config' =>  \r                     'test' => true,\r                  \r              ,\r          \r      ->getfeedio  ;\r    \r you get  php fatal error:  uncaught exception 'reflectionexception' with message 'class feedio\\factory\\builder\\guzzleclientbuilder does not have a constructor, so you cannot pass any constructor arguments'  . there is no workaround for that.\r \r     discussion\r i can easily fix this but i need your acknowledgement that it's an oversight.

title: unix domain socket crashes program
body: i get a program crash while using unix domain sockets.\r \r i am still working on a redis client. while adding unix domain support, i found that crystal crashes my program for larger messages.\r \r this program shows the issue  requires a redis server with a unix domain socket at /tmp/redis.sock :\r \r    crystal\r require \ socket\ \r \r io = unixsocket.new \ /tmp/redis.sock\  \r 6.times do |i|\r   io << \  3\\r\ \ \r   io << \ $3\\r\ \ \r   io << \ set\ \r   io << \ $ {i.to_s.length}\\r\ \ \r   io << \ foo\\r\ \ \r   io << \  {i.to_s}\\r\ \ \r end\r io.flush\r    \r \r i get 3 different behaviours, completely random.\r \r first, a simple crash:\r    \r $ crystal run experiments/unixsocket-hanging.cr\r using compiled compiler at .build/crystal\r program terminated abnormally with error code: 13\r    \r \r second, a crash with stacktrace:\r    \r $ crystal run experiments/unixsocket-hanging.cr\r using compiled compiler at .build/crystal\r error writing file: socket is not connected  errno \r  exception@exception initialize<errno, string>:array string  +46  4461984222 \r  errno initialize<errno, string>:array string  +128  4461984160 \r  errno::new<string>:errno +119  4461984007 \r  unixsocket@filedescriptorio write<unixsocket, slice uint8 , int32>:nil +247  4461983495 \r  unixsocket@io write<unixsocket, slice uint8 >:nil +61  4461983229 \r  string to_s<string, unixsocket>:nil +90  4461971946 \r  unixsocket@io <<<unixsocket, string>:unixsocket +22  4461983142 \r __crystal_main +864  4461961392 \r main +32  4461963264 \r program terminated abnormally with error code: 256\r    \r \r third, the program terminates seemingly successfully:\r    \r $ crystal run experiments/unixsocket-hanging.cr\r using compiled compiler at .build/crystal\r    \r \r \r \r \r \r \r \r \r \r

title: tags in s3 lifecycle_rule not supported
body: _this issue was originally opened by @timboo89 as hashicorp/terraform 16153. it was migrated here as a result of the  provider split  https://www.hashicorp.com/blog/upcoming-provider-changes-in-terraform-0-10/ . the original body of the issue is below._\r \r <hr>\r \r     terraform version\r 0.10.2\r \r     terraform configuration files\r    hcl\r resource \ aws_s3_bucket\  \ bucket\  {\r   bucket = \ my-bucket\ \r   acl    = \ private\ \r \r   lifecycle_rule {\r     id      = \ log\ \r     enabled = true\r \r     tags {\r       \ rule\       = \ log\ \r       \ retention\  = \ 403\ \r     }\r \r     transition {\r       days          = 403\r       storage_class = \ glacier\ \r     }\r   }\r }\r    \r \r     expected behavior\r create a bucket with a lifecycle rule thats moves files after 403 days to glacier.\r \r     actual behavior\r just creates a simple s3 bucket without lifecycle rules.\r \r     steps to reproduce\r 1.  terraform init \r 2.  terraform apply \r \r     work-a-round\r just don't use tags in lifecycle rules:\r    hcl\r resource \ aws_s3_bucket\  \ bucket\  {\r   bucket = \ my-bucket\ \r   acl    = \ private\ \r \r   lifecycle_rule {\r     id      = \ log\ \r     enabled = true\r \r     transition {\r       days          = 403\r       storage_class = \ glacier\ \r     }\r   }\r }\r    \r \r \r \r

title: fix non-standard 'include' location for exodusii build.
body: jed brown pointed out that bv_exodus.sh puts installed include directory in a non-standard location. that's because bv_exodus.sh puts it in a non-standard place for no apparent reason: cp ../include/ .h \ $visitdir/exodus/$exodus_version/$visitarch/inc\  any normal install will use 'include'. we should fix bv_exodus.sh, and modify our findexodusii.cmake file to use 'include' instead of 'inc' in the call to set_up_third_party.      -----------------------redmine migration----------------------- this ticket was migrated from redmine. as such, not all  information was able to be captured in the transition. below is a complete record of the original redmine ticket.  ticket number: 1400 status: pending project: visit tracker: bug priority: normal subject: fix non-standard 'include' location for exodusii build. assigned to:  category:  target version:  author: kathleen biagas start: 03/28/2013 due date:  % done: 0 estimated time:  created: 03/28/2013 01:49 pm updated: 04/15/2013 01:44 pm likelihood: 3 - occasional severity: 2 - minor irritation found in version: 2.6.1 impact:  expected use:  os: all support group: any description:  jed brown pointed out that bv_exodus.sh puts installed include directory in a non-standard location. that's because bv_exodus.sh puts it in a non-standard place for no apparent reason: cp ../include/ .h \ $visitdir/exodus/$exodus_version/$visitarch/inc\  any normal install will use 'include'. we should fix bv_exodus.sh, and modify our findexodusii.cmake file to use 'include' instead of 'inc' in the call to set_up_third_party.  comments:

title: dev  xdcp from non-root user copies files as root by default
body: in the following example,  vhu  is xcat enabled non-root user.       vhu@fs2vm100 ~ $ xdcp fs2vm101 /tmp/victor_file /tmp/testcopy/  vhu@fs2vm100 ~ $ xdsh fs2vm101 ls -l /tmp/testcopy fs2vm101: total 0 fs2vm101: -rw-r--r-- 1 root root 0 apr 28 11:46 victor_file      work around is to use  -l  option to specify user       vhu@fs2vm100 ~ $ xdcp fs2vm101 -l vhu /tmp/victor_file /tmp/testcopy/  vhu@fs2vm100 ~ $ xdsh fs2vm101 ls -l /tmp/testcopy fs2vm101: total 0 fs2vm101: -rw-r--r-- 1 vhu users 0 apr 28 11:48 victor_file      @daniceexi  should xdcp issued from non-root user preserve user by default?
text: dev  xdcp from non-root user copies files as root by default. in the following example,  vhu  is xcat enabled non-root user.       vhu@fs2vm100 ~ $ xdcp fs2vm101 /tmp/victor_file /tmp/testcopy/  vhu@fs2vm100 ~ $ xdsh fs2vm101 ls -l /tmp/testcopy fs2vm101: total 0 fs2vm101: -rw-r--r-- 1 root root 0 apr 28 11:46 victor_file      work around is to use  -l  option to specify user       vhu@fs2vm100 ~ $ xdcp fs2vm101 -l vhu /tmp/victor_file /tmp/testcopy/  vhu@fs2vm100 ~ $ xdsh fs2vm101 ls -l /tmp/testcopy fs2vm101: total 0 fs2vm101: -rw-r--r-- 1 vhu users 0 apr 28 11:48 victor_file      @daniceexi  should xdcp issued from non-root user preserve user by default?

title: nlp.pipe returning slightly modified word tags
body: the bug\r i'm encountering a bug where nlp.pipe behaves differently on subsequent passes through the same text generator. the tag discrepancies appear to be rare, but nevertheless are there. \r \r    info about spacy\r \r     spacy version:   1.8.2\r     platform:   linux-4.11.9-1-arch-x86_64-with-arch\r     python version:   3.6.1\r     installed models:   \r \r    code which reproduces the bug\r    \r  !/usr/bin/env python3\r \r import pickle\r from pathlib import path\r \r import en_core_web_sm as en\r \r nlp = en.load  \r \r \r class readtext:\r     def __init__ self :\r         self.file_paths = path \r                 '/home/moreland/ai/wikicorpus-small/'\r                  .glob ' .p' \r \r     def __iter__ self :\r         for filename in sorted self.file_paths :\r             print 'reading: {}'.format filename  \r             with open filename, 'rb'  as f:\r                 for article in pickle.load f :\r                     yield article\r \r \r class corpus:\r     def __iter__ self :\r         for article in nlp.pipe \r                 readtext  ,\r                 batch_size=1000,\r                 n_threads=4 :\r \r             yield article\r    \r \r first let's test the text generator to make sure we can loop through it multiple times and retrieve the same object. this test passes without any problems.\r    \r gen_first =  text for text in readtext   \r gen_second =  text for text in readtext   \r \r   this passes  text generator can be repeated \r assert gen_first == gen_second\r    \r \r next let's test that nlp.pipe is deterministic, i.e., that it produces the same object given the same inputs. first we test that it contains the same text  this passes . then we can check that it produces the same part-of-speech tags  this fails .\r    \r doc_first =  d for d in corpus   \r doc_second =  d for d in corpus   \r \r   this passes  document text is the same \r assert str doc_first  == str doc_second \r \r tags_first =   \r for article in doc_first:\r     for sentence in article.sents:\r         tags_first.extend  w.tag_ for w in sentence  \r \r tags_second=   \r for article in doc_second:\r     for sentence in article.sents:\r         tags_second.extend  w.tag_ for w in sentence  \r \r   this does not pass. some tags change on the second pass?\r assert tags_first == tags_second\r    \r \r    what's broken\r   unfortunately,  tags_first != tags_second  although they should be the same!  \r \r the tag which flipped was  word.tag_ = 'md'  on the first pass and  word.tag_ = 'uh'  on the second pass.

appear to be rare, but nevertheless are there. \r \r    info about spacy\r \r     spacy version:   1.8.2\r     platform:   linux-4.11.9-1-arch-x86_64-with-arch\r     python version:   3.6.1\r     installed models:   \r \r    code which reproduces the bug\r    \r  !/usr/bin/env python3\r \r import pickle\r from pathlib import path\r \r import en_core_web_sm as en\r \r nlp = en.load  \r \r \r class readtext:\r     def __init__ self :\r         self.file_paths = path \r                 '/home/moreland/ai/wikicorpus-small/'\r                  .glob ' .p' \r \r     def __iter__ self :\r n_threads=4 :\r \r             yield article\r    \r \r first let's test the text generator to make sure we can loop through it multiple times and retrieve the same object. this test passes without any problems.\r    \r gen_first =  text for text in readtext   \r gen_second =  text for text in readtext   \r \r   this passes  text generator can be repeated \r assert gen_first == gen_second\r    \r \r next let's test that nlp.pipe is deterministic, i.e., that it produces the same object given the same inputs. first we test that it contains the same text  this passes . then we can check that it produces the same part-of-speech tags  this fails .\r    \r doc_first =  d for d in corpus   \r doc_second =  d for d in corpus   \r \r   this passes  document text is the same \r assert str doc_first  == str doc_second \r \r tags_first =   \r for article in doc_first:\r     for sentence in article.sents:\r         tags_first.extend  w.tag_ for w in sentence  \r \r tags_second=   \r for article in doc_second:\r     for sentence in article.sents:\r         tags_second.extend  w.tag_ for w in sentence  \r \r   this does not pass. some tags change on the second pass?\r assert tags_first title: camerarig.reset   et al not working as intended?
body: i want to be able to manually perform a reorientation, claim that the direction i'm currently looking in is the normal forward. it looks like the code to do this is something like  mgvrcontext.getmainscene  .getmaincamerarig  .reset     or resetyaw for just the yaw . calling this doesn't appear to have any effect.\r \r test app: gearvrf-demo/gvr-modelviewer with the following addition to actually call onbackpressed upon pressing back.  similar lack of behavior exhibited by the other two test apps that call resetyaw. \r \r     @override\r     public boolean onkeyup int keycode, keyevent event  {\r         if event.getkeycode   == keyevent.keycode_back {\r             onbackpressed  ;\r             return true;\r         }\r         return super.onkeyup keycode, event ;\r     }\r \r  changes also at https://github.com/imuli/gearvrf-demos/tree/reset-bug-example
text: camerarig.reset   et al not working as intended?. i want to be able to manually perform a reorientation, claim that the direction i'm currently looking in is the normal forward. it looks like the code to do this is something like  mgvrcontext.getmainscene  .getmaincamerarig  .reset     or resetyaw for just the yaw . calling this doesn't appear to have any effect.\r \r test app: gearvrf-demo/gvr-modelviewer with the following addition to actually call onbackpressed upon pressing back.  similar lack of behavior exhibited by the other two test apps that call resetyaw. \r \r     @override\r     public boolean onkeyup int keycode, keyevent event  {\r         if event.getkeycode   == keyevent.keycode_back {\r             onbackpressed  ;\r             return true;\r         }\r         return super.onkeyup keycode, event ;\r     }\r \r  changes also at https://github.com/imuli/gearvrf-demos/tree/reset-bug-example

title: changing languages a few times breaks because of too many audiocontexts
body: changing languages several times causes this error, which totally stops your project from being used.\r \r ! image  https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/654102/28466873-d365e188-6dfb-11e7-8d19-fab26be4fce7.png \r \r > uncaught domexception: failed to construct 'audiocontext': the number of hardware contexts provided  6  is greater than or equal to the maximum bound  6 .\r \r this is actually being caused by the  audioengine  from scratch-audio, because the audio stuff in the gui has a  sharedaudiocontext  utility to only ever create one audio context. a similar strategy could be used on the audio engine.
text: changing languages a few times breaks because of too many audiocontexts. changing languages several times causes this error, which totally stops your project from being used.\r \r ! image  https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/654102/28466873-d365e188-6dfb-11e7-8d19-fab26be4fce7.png \r \r > uncaught domexception: failed to construct 'audiocontext': the number of hardware contexts provided  6  is greater than or equal to the maximum bound  6 .\r \r this is actually being caused by the  audioengine  from scratch-audio, because the audio stuff in the gui has a  sharedaudiocontext  utility to only ever create one audio context. a similar strategy could be used on the audio engine.

title: navbuttonforward unlocked on last question
body: description\r \r the current logic for the locked/unlocked state of the navbuttonforward will evaluate to locked when the user is on the last question and it does not have a response but change to unlocked once the user answers it.\r \r below is a step by step of the conditions and behaviour.\r \r scenario\r \r given a user is on the last question of the survey\r and the last question does not have a response\r \r \r \r \r \r __current behaviour__\r \r when the user answers the last question\r then the forward nav button becomes unlocked\r \r \r \r \r \r __expected behaviour__\r \r when the user answers the last question\r then the forward nav button remains in a locked state\r
text: navbuttonforward unlocked on last question. description\r \r the current logic for the locked/unlocked state of the navbuttonforward will evaluate to locked when the user is on the last question and it does not have a response but change to unlocked once the user answers it.\r \r below is a step by step of the conditions and behaviour.\r \r scenario\r \r given a user is on the last question of the survey\r and the last question does not have a response\r \r \r \r \r \r __current behaviour__\r \r when the user answers the last question\r then the forward nav button becomes unlocked\r \r \r \r \r \r __expected behaviour__\r \r when the user answers the last question\r then the forward nav button remains in a locked state\r

title: git-lfs will not use sslcainfo or urlmatch
body: slightly related to  276, but git-lfs does not support sslcainfo, so if i have a ca to point to, no dice.\r \r further more it does not support \ urlmatch\  for additional http in the config either, so if in my global ~/.gitconfig i...\r \r    \r  http \r     sslverify = false\r    \r this works of course, \r \r i can not\r    \r  http \ https://only.one.site.com/\  \r     sslverify = false\r    \r i think this it mainly due to b94361f1f001011e5312d0de2db9ae3376f6a9a3 git-lfs/lfs/http.go line: 41, but that's my 5 second guess\r \r needless to say, \r    \r  http \ https://only.one.site.com/\  \r     sslcainfo = /home/me/.ssl/trusted.crt\r    \r won't work either.

title: content of media-queries is always output two times
body: our  .response  mixin is buggy, as it outputs all media-queries for breakpoints  large ,  small , etc. two times. consider the following code.\r \r    less\r .foo {\r   .respond large, {\r     display: block;\r   } ;\r }\r    \r \r compiles to:\r \r    css\r @media  max-width: large  {\r   .foo {\r     display: block;\r   }\r }\r \r @media  max-width: 960px  {\r   .foo {\r     display: block;\r   }\r }\r    \r \r this is because of less's mixin pattern matching feature: http://lesscss.org/features/ mixins-parametric-feature-pattern-matching\r \r i'm working on a fix with guards, we can save a lot of css by fixing that.
text: content of media-queries is always output two times. our  .response  mixin is buggy, as it outputs all media-queries for breakpoints  large ,  small , etc. two times. consider the following code.\r \r    less\r .foo {\r   .respond large, {\r     display: block;\r   } ;\r }\r    \r \r compiles to:\r \r    css\r @media  max-width: large  {\r   .foo {\r     display: block;\r   }\r }\r \r @media  max-width: 960px  {\r   .foo {\r     display: block;\r   }\r }\r    \r \r this is because of less's mixin pattern matching feature: http://lesscss.org/features/ mixins-parametric-feature-pattern-matching\r \r i'm working on a fix with guards, we can save a lot of css by fixing that.

title: \ list a venue\  form leads nowhere
body: one of our team tried to list a new venue, and we found out that the process ends after the first form - rather than going to a profile page that can be edited, it just goes to the public view, with no option to add any more details or upload a photo. this may have to do with the admins now only having access to the back-end?\r \r
text: \ list a venue\  form leads nowhere. one of our team tried to list a new venue, and we found out that the process ends after the first form - rather than going to a profile page that can be edited, it just goes to the public view, with no option to add any more details or upload a photo. this may have to do with the admins now only having access to the back-end?\r \r

title: setstate warning using v5.0.0-rc.1 with redux-form.
body: the following error appears using  react-redux@5.0.0-rc.1  when i am unmounting component which renders  redux-form 's  <field />  component only if the  <field />  has been previously focused. i know that it seems like problem with  redux-form  but when i switch back to  react-redux@4.4.6  everything works fine.\r \r    \r warning.js:36 warning: setstate ... : can only update a mounted or mounting component. this usually means you called setstate   on an unmounted component. this is a no-op. please check the code for the connect connectedfield  component.\r    \r \r tested on osx and chrome@54.0.2840.98  64-bit  and safari@10.0.1  12602.2.14.0.7 \r \r how to reproduce\r ---\r \r 1. clone the repository  https://github.com/pilaas/react-redux-bug \r 1.  yarn \r 1.  npm run start \r 1. open browser  http://localhost:8080 \r 1. open console and enter type  store.dispatch {type: 'show_form'} ; \r 1. focus newly appeared input component\r 1. enter  store.dispatch {type: 'hide_form'} ;  in console\r 1. experience warning\r
text: setstate warning using v5.0.0-rc.1 with redux-form.. the following error appears using  react-redux@5.0.0-rc.1  when i am unmounting component which renders  redux-form 's  <field />  component only if the  <field />  has been previously focused. i know that it seems like problem with  redux-form  but when i switch back to  react-redux@4.4.6  everything works fine.\r \r    \r warning.js:36 warning: setstate ... : can only update a mounted or mounting component. this usually means you called setstate   on an unmounted component. this is a no-op. please check the code for the connect connectedfield  component.\r    \r \r tested on osx and chrome@54.0.2840.98  64-bit  and safari@10.0.1  12602.2.14.0.7 \r \r how to reproduce\r ---\r \r 1. clone the repository  https://github.com/pilaas/react-redux-bug \r 1.  yarn \r 1.  npm run start \r 1. open browser  http://localhost:8080 \r 1. open console and enter type  store.dispatch {type: 'show_form'} ; \r 1. focus newly appeared input component\r 1. enter  store.dispatch {type: 'hide_form'} ;  in console\r 1. experience warning\r

title: serenity cucumber does not scan @manual on feature level
body: hi, in the document, it states that tagging a feature file @manual on feature  story  level would consider all scenarios under the file manual scenarios.  > generally, it is possible to use it to mark scenarios  @manual on scenario level  or all scenarios in story  @manual on story level .  however, from what i've tested, it seems the report does not properly recognize scenarios on a file with @manual on top of the file as manual scenarios. the test steps are not skipped; and there is no humanoid icon for the manual scenario on the report. could you introduce a fix for this issue? the workaround is to tag @manual above each scenario. i'm using serenity cucumber version 1.1.11

title: 1.12  some goats seem to have infinite milk.
body: expected behavior\r i'd assume you aren't supposed to be able to get infinite buckets of milk one after another.\r \r     actual behavior\r right-clicking some goats give infinite milk.\r \r     steps to reproduce\r for me, i found two goats in the wild that i could milk infinitely. both were angora goats. i don't know if they gave birth in the wild or not, but not all angora goats did it, only two that i found. i did not manually feed or water the goats.\r \r     version of minecraft, version of animania, single player or server\r minecraft 1.12.2, animania 1.4.7, forge 14.23.2.2654, both single player and server.\r \r     screenshots encouraged\r ! 2018-04-05_13 54 29  https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6501298/38391428-07dfca02-38d9-11e8-81d4-20f0267f984a.png \r

title: does not remember monitor in multi-monitor-setup

title: preprocessing stalling gpu
body: right now the code is set to do preprocessing on a single thread and then passing batches to the gpu. looks like there's something with the gil not being released quickly enough or the preprocessing taking too long relative to the time to do the gpu compute. spawing more preprocessing threads doesn't solve the issue, so it's not an io bottleneck. this is a nontrivial issue that might require looking into multitprocess preprocessing, rather than threading.

title: smooth.m edge effects
body: just found an issue with smooth.m when it handles linear data.  it prepends/appends the flipped data, to a vector, then smooths.  this works well for circular data, but causes linear variables to greatly diverge from their inputs.  run these two for comparison:\r \r  smooth  1 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 ,2  \r  smooth  0 0 0 0 1 0 0 0 0 ,2  \r \r will fix in the dev branch this week, and close this when it's pred to master

title: --service-node and --service-cluster are not used when sending discoveryrequest
body: description :\r when envoy is run with --service-node and --service-cluster cli parameters the values are not respected when sending xds discoveryrequests. if node element is present in the bootstrap file the values from bootstrap are sent, if node element is not present in bootstrap file node.id and node.cluster in discoveryrequest are empty.\r \r expected behavior is that value passed to --service-node is used as node.id in all discoveryrequests and --service-cluster is used as node.cluster.\r \r  repro steps :\r given envoy is run with the following command\r    envoy --service-cluster cluster0 --service-node _node_0 -c envoy.yaml   \r and node element is not present in envoy.yaml file, all xds request are snet with following node definition:\r    \r sending discoveryrequest for type.googleapis.com/envoy.api.v2.cluster: node {\r    build_version: \ 3f59fb5c0f6554f8b3f2e73ab4c1437a63d42668/1.7.0/clean/release\ \r }\r

title: performance issue with  main.tile  mask creation
body: there is a bug in rasterio  mentioned in https://github.com/mapbox/rasterio/issues/1191, https://github.com/mapbox/rasterio/issues/1266  wrappedvrt which might be linked in poor performance when reading  mask  withing a file. \r \r for sentinel, landsat and cbers we don't read the mask because we are creating one using a nodata value.
text: performance issue with  main.tile  mask creation. there is a bug in rasterio  mentioned in https://github.com/mapbox/rasterio/issues/1191, https://github.com/mapbox/rasterio/issues/1266  wrappedvrt which might be linked in poor performance when reading  mask  withing a file. \r \r for sentinel, landsat and cbers we don't read the mask because we are creating one using a nodata value.

title: labelcollection incorrectly caches clamped position when re-using
body: the below code:\r \r 1. creates a label,  abc , with a position clamped to ground.\r 2. when you press  step 1 , it removes the label \r 3. when you press  step 2 , it adds a new label,  abcd , without a position.\r \r the bug is that the second label should not be displayed  because it has no position , but the first 3 letters shows up at the first removed label's position because the glyphs still have a value for  _actualclampedposition .  i'm not sure if this is a bug in  labelcollection  or a bug in  billboardcollection  being exposed via label usage.\r \r    javascript\r var viewer = new cesium.viewer 'cesiumcontainer' ;\r \r viewer.terrainprovider = new cesium.cesiumterrainprovider {\r     url : 'https://assets.agi.com/stk-terrain/world',\r     requestwatermask : true,\r     requestvertexnormals : true\r } ;\r viewer.scene.globe.depthtestagainstterrain = true;\r \r var labels = new cesium.labelcollection {scene:viewer.scene} ;\r viewer.scene.primitives.add labels ;\r \r var firstlabel = labels.add {\r     position : cesium.cartesian3.fromdegrees 0,0 ,\r     text : 'abc',\r     font : '24px helvetica',\r     heightreference : cesium.heightreference.clamp_to_ground\r } ;\r \r sandcastle.addtoolbarbutton 'step1', function  {\r     labels.remove firstlabel ;\r } ;\r \r sandcastle.addtoolbarbutton 'step2', function  {\r     var secondlabel = labels.add {\r         text : 'defg',\r         font : '24px helvetica',\r         heightreference : cesium.heightreference.clamp_to_ground\r     } ;\r } ;\r    \r \r

title: items in an excluded folder are still indexed
body: when you exclude a folder in the project, it still indexes the files; this can take a huge amount of time on large projects with many assets  thousands of files .  even with a larger heap size it can take forever to index, because it is trying to index files that you are trying to exclude.  ideally, scanning files should ignore excluded folders so this step can be much shorter.

title: 21220820: cfurlcreatestringbyaddingpercentescapes documentation states incorrect rfc spec
body: description  summary:\r the documentation for  cfurlcreatestringbyaddingpercentescapes  states:\r \r > the characters escaped are all characters that are not legal url characters  based on rfc 3986 , plus any characters in legalurlcharacterstobeescaped, less any characters in characterstoleaveunescaped. to simply correct any non-url characters in an otherwise correct url string, pass null for the allocator, characterstoleaveescaped, and legalurlcharacterstobeescaped parameters, and kcfstringencodingutf8 as the encoding parameter.\r \r - https://developer.apple.com/library/mac/documentation/corefoundation/reference/cfurlref/ //apple_ref/c/func/cfurlcreatestringbyaddingpercentescapes\r \r the header documentation states the following:\r \r > creates a copy or originalstring, replacing certain characters with the equivalent percent escape sequence based on the encoding specified. if the originalstring does not need to be modified  no percent escape sequences are missing , may retain and return originalstring. if you are uncertain of the correct encoding, you should use utf-8, which is the encoding designated by rfc 2396 as the correct encoding for use in urls.  the characters so escaped are all characters that are not legal url characters  based on rfc 2396 , plus any characters in legalurlcharacterstobeescaped, less any characters in characterstoleaveunescaped.\r \r as you can see, the public documentation believes the percent escaping follows rfc 3986 while the header claims to follow rfc 2396. though various tests, it has been observed that  cfurlcreatestringbyaddingpercentescapes  does follow rfc 2396 as stated in the header. the documentation should be updated to reflect this fact.\r \r here is a chunk of swift code to demonstrate the issue.\r \r    \r func escape string: string  -> string {\r     return cfurlcreatestringbyaddingpercentescapes nil, string, nil, nil, cfstringbuiltinencodings.utf8.rawvalue  as string\r }\r \r let asciicharacters =  \r     \  \ , \ !\ , \ \\\ \ , \  \ , \ $\ , \ %\ , \ &\ , \ '\ , \  \ , \  \ , \  \ , \ +\ , \ ,\ , \ -\ , \ .\ , \ /\ , \ :\ ,\r     \ ;\ , \ <\ , \ =\ , \ >\ , \ ?\ , \ @\ , \  \ , \ \\\\\ , \  \ , \ ^\ , \ _\ , \  \ , \ {\ , \ |\ , \ }\ , \ ~\ ,\r     \ 0123456789\ , \ abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz\ , \ abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz\ \r  \r \r asciicharacters.map { println \ \\\ \\ $0 \\\  = \\\ \\ escape $0  \\\ \   }\r \r let nonlegalcharacters = \  \\\  %<>  \\\\^ {}|\ \r    \r \r the following characters are considered \ legal\  in rfc 3986.\r \r - \    \ \r \r steps to reproduce:\r \r \r expected results:\r \r \r actual results:\r \r \r version:\r \r \r notes:\r \r \r configuration:\r \r \r attachments:  - product version: xcode 6.3.2 created: 2015-06-03t15:38:02.576250 originated: 2015-06-03t08:35:00 open radar link: http://www.openradar.me/21220820

title: messageport: call start when onmessage is activated, hide start
body: when a message is posted to one port of a  messagechannel , it never seems to be delivered to the other port. the following test reproduces this:      dart import 'dart:html';  import 'package:js/js.dart'; import 'package:test/test.dart';  @js \ eval\   external _eval string js ;  void main   {   test \ a message can be passed through a channel\ ,    async {     var channel = _eval \ new messagechannel  \   as messagechannel;     channel.port1.postmessage \ hello\  ;     print await channel.port2.onmessage.first ;   } ; }      note that the eval trick is there to work around  26349. this test never completes in dartium, chrome, or firefox. the corresponding raw js works as expected:      js var channel = new messagechannel  ; channel.port1.postmessage \ hello\  ; channel.port2.onmessage = function message  { console.log message ; }
text: messageport: call start when onmessage is activated, hide start. when a message is posted to one port of a  messagechannel , it never seems to be delivered to the other port. the following test reproduces this:      dart import 'dart:html';  import 'package:js/js.dart'; import 'package:test/test.dart';  @js \ eval\   external _eval string js ;  void main   {   test \ a message can be passed through a channel\ ,    async {     var channel = _eval \ new messagechannel  \   as messagechannel;     channel.port1.postmessage \ hello\  ;     print await channel.port2.onmessage.first ;   } ; }      note that the eval trick is there to work around  26349. this test never completes in dartium, chrome, or firefox. the corresponding raw js works as expected:      js var channel = new messagechannel  ; channel.port1.postmessage \ hello\  ; channel.port2.onmessage = function message  { console.log message ; }

title: unclassified flybase phenotype terms
body:
some flybase phenotypes are not mapped to their relevant fbbt or any other ontology classes.\r \r for example. there's an annotation between a genotype and the phenotype adult abdomen & macrochaeta | anterior compartment .\r \r i _think_ the general pattern is if a phenotype label is delimited with ampersands, then it isn't mapped to cvterms, but if it is delimited by pipes, it is.\r \r for these cases, our pipeline currently creates a blank node for this term, using the internal flybase key to make a temporary id, like: \r 
\r :_fbcvtermkey89720phenotype a owl:class ;\r     rdfs:label \ adult abdomen & macrochaeta | anterior compartment phenotype\  .\r
\r this is clearly suboptimal. ideally we'd like to get these fixed at the source, and for flybase to maybe use upheno, if we create relevant classes.\r \r @dosumis may know why this is, and @cmungall may want to speculate on upheno integration, and @jmcmurry w.r.t. the id generation.

unclassified flybase phenotype terms. some flybase phenotypes are not mapped to their relevant fbbt or any other ontology classes.\r \r for example. there's an annotation between a genotype and the phenotype adult abdomen & macrochaeta | anterior compartment .\r \r i _think_ the general pattern is if a phenotype label is delimited with ampersands, then it isn't mapped to cvterms, but if it is delimited by pipes, it is.\r \r for these cases, our pipeline currently creates a blank node for this term, using the internal flybase key to make a temporary id, like: \r 
\r :_fbcvtermkey89720phenotype a owl:class ;\r     rdfs:label \ adult abdomen & macrochaeta | anterior compartment phenotype\  .\r
\r this is clearly suboptimal. ideally we'd like to get these fixed at the source, and for flybase to maybe use upheno, if we create relevant classes.\r \r @dosumis may know why this is, and @cmungall may want to speculate on upheno integration, and @jmcmurry w.r.t. the id generation.

title: options must be idempotent

title: documentation: minor typo in the window guide
body: the last paragraph in the  \ full screen windows\   http://www.glfw.org/docs/latest/window_guide.html  section states as follows:\r \r > by default, the original video mode of the monitor will be restored and the window iconified if it loses input focus, to allow the user to switch back to the desktop. this behavior can be disabled with the glfw_auto_iconify window hint, for example if you wish to simultaneously cover multiple windows with full screen windows.\r \r i believe last words were meant to be written as\r \r > cover multiple _monitors_ with full screen windows.\r \r instead of \r \r > cover multiple _windows_ with full screen windows.\r \r cheers, and thanks for all your work! you are doing great things here.

title: remove incomplete end folds for ts cross-validation
body: don't like having these included. statistics calculated off these will have higher variance due to fewer observations. could overly influence results. only compare folds with same number of observations in validation set.

title: missing lvm snapshot volume results in data loss  r4-rc1
body: r4-rc1 plus some updates from testing\r \r while attempting to install windows 7 in a standalone hvm, i performed many start/stop/kill cycles which possibly got things into a strange state.  for whatever reason, my win7 root volume was missing its snapshot volume when it should have been there.  the only manual lvm command that i had executed was the gui-recommended lvm resize on the root volume about an hour earlier, and then performed several more stop/start cycles while installing win7 patches.\r \r after a later \ stop\  command, i lost my root volume completely and was unable to start the vm again.  i believe that thinvolume::stop called thinvolume::_commit which does not check that the snapshot volume  self._vid_snap  exists  i.e. is_dirty  before removing the main volume  self.vid  and cloning the snapshot.\r \r https://github.com/qubesos/qubes-core-admin/blob/b8d45c214d96516f807fa3d4f9f4bb133b1ef4d6/qubes/storage/lvm.py l274\r \r i believe that adding a check here would prevent anyone who gets into this weird state from losing data in the future.  thank you.   -doug
text: missing lvm snapshot volume results in data loss  r4-rc1. r4-rc1 plus some updates from testing\r \r while attempting to install windows 7 in a standalone hvm, i performed many start/stop/kill cycles which possibly got things into a strange state.  for whatever reason, my win7 root volume was missing its snapshot volume when it should have been there.  the only manual lvm command that i had executed was the gui-recommended lvm resize on the root volume about an hour earlier, and then performed several more stop/start cycles while installing win7 patches.\r \r after a later \ stop\  command, i lost my root volume completely and was unable to start the vm again.  i believe that thinvolume::stop called thinvolume::_commit which does not check that the snapshot volume  self._vid_snap  exists  i.e. is_dirty  before removing the main volume  self.vid  and cloning the snapshot.\r \r https://github.com/qubesos/qubes-core-admin/blob/b8d45c214d96516f807fa3d4f9f4bb133b1ef4d6/qubes/storage/lvm.py l274\r \r i believe that adding a check here would prevent anyone who gets into this weird state from losing data in the future.  thank you.   -doug

title: json output contains redundant font-related styles.
body: two problems:\r \r - font styles don't seem to propagate up to the parent, so each child receives them, even if the child has nothing to do with typography\r - the deprecated  font  property is exported along with the newer  fontfamily  that replaces it. this one's easy to fix.\r \r e.g.\r    json\r   \ shape\ , {\r     \ applymatrix\ : false,\r     \ matrix\ :   1, 0, 0, 1, 193.5, 141.5  ,\r     \ clipmask\ : true,\r     \ type\ : \ rectangle\ ,\r     \ size\ :   113, 113  ,\r     \ radius\ :   0, 0  ,\r     \ fillcolor\ : null,\r     \ strokecolor\ : null,\r     \ miterlimit\ : 4,\r     \ fontfamily\ : \ 'lucida grande', geneva, verdana, arial, sans-serif\ ,\r     \ fontsize\ : 13,\r     \ font\ : \ 'lucida grande', geneva, verdana, arial, sans-serif\ ,\r     \ leading\ : 15.6\r }  \r

title: search in large directories causes the system to freeze
body: ripgrep uses all available system resources when something is searched within a very large directory, e.g. root  / .\r it takes up the 16gb ram of my machine instantly which causes it to freeze until the process is killed.\r here is the example call:\r \r    sh\r $ rg '$somevar' / 2&>/dev/null\r zsh: killed     rg '$somevar' / 2&> /dev/null\r    \r \r i am using the following ripgrep build:\r \r    sh\r $ rg --version\r ripgrep 0.3.2\r

title: jdbccodelist improperly updates the code list map registered in request
body: description\r the code list map already registered by the  codelistinterceptor  in request is updated by the  jdbccodelist refresh .\r \r     problem sequence  in 1 request \r 1.   codelistinterceptor prehandle   https://github.com/terasolunaorg/terasoluna-gfw/blob/c0ce210e665f774903115a71ab663ac7e2e465e1/terasoluna-gfw-common-libraries/terasoluna-gfw-web/src/main/java/org/terasoluna/gfw/web/codelist/codelistinterceptor.java l91-l114  register the code list map in request.\r   code list map ->  {a, value1}   old \r 2. handler method   @controller   update database and execute   jdbccodelist refrash   https://github.com/terasolunaorg/terasoluna-gfw/blob/c0ce210e665f774903115a71ab663ac7e2e465e1/terasoluna-gfw-common-libraries/terasoluna-gfw-common/src/main/java/org/terasoluna/gfw/common/codelist/abstractreloadablecodelist.java l81-l90 .\r   code list map ->  {a, value2}   new \r   ex.  {a, value2}   new code list map \r 3. view  ex. jsp  get code list map from request.\r   code list map ->  {a, value2}     not old, but new!!!   \r \r     problem details\r if registering in a request with the  codelistinterceptor prehandle , it is strange that the map is updated by handler operation. if you want to get the updated map, you should register in the request with the  posthandle .\r \r     problem cause\r the mechanism by which the map already registered in request is updated is as follows\r \r https://github.com/terasolunaorg/terasoluna-gfw/blob/c0ce210e665f774903115a71ab663ac7e2e465e1/terasoluna-gfw-common-libraries/terasoluna-gfw-common/src/main/java/org/terasoluna/gfw/common/codelist/abstractreloadablecodelist.java l81-l90\r \r  abstractcodelist refrash  just updating  cachedmap  contents instead of reassigning instance.\r and  collections.unmodifiablemap  creates an unmodifiable map based on the map, but reflects changes to the original map...\r \r    possible solutions\r for examle, one way of the following.\r   stop  cachedmap.clearandputall  and reassign instance.\r   register the request with  codelistinterceptor posthandle  instead of  prehandle .\r \r     caution\r if you fix this problem...\r since there may be a project that is being developed on the basis of the current behavior, it is necessary to be careful about the announcement at the time of change.\r \r    affects version/s\r - 5.x.x.release\r - 1.x.x.release\r \r    fix version/s\r  to be written later by project member \r -     5.5.0  master \r -     5.4.2  5.4.x \r \r    final solution\r  to be written later by project member \r \r    migrating guide\r -     required\r \r    issue links\r -  xxx

title: inconsistent handling of html tags in topic and knowledge
body:
if i specify the topics with html tags such as

topic 1

,

topic 2

etc and use the same for the list of knowledge questions, the behaviour is inconsistent.\r \r knowledge questions seem to be handled differently from topics.\r things look differently in the preview and then later in actual usage.\r \r maybe you want to simply consider all these fields as plain text fields and always use the s function when printing them via php, and the appropriate way to handle them via js.

title: using the mutate filter's date conversion on an empty field results in a date at the beginning of 1970
body: if an empty field is given to the  convert  feature on the  mutate  filter, the result is  1970-01-01t00:00:00.000z . this can give misleading results so it's best to just leave the original value intact.
text: using the mutate filter's date conversion on an empty field results in a date at the beginning of 1970. if an empty field is given to the  convert  feature on the  mutate  filter, the result is  1970-01-01t00:00:00.000z . this can give misleading results so it's best to just leave the original value intact.

title: change retrievedocsforreader job to limit requests made by efolder to vbms
body: before efolder is enabled for reader, the retrievedocumentsforreader batch job needs to be adjusted to limit the number of requests made by efolder to vbms.\r \r the batch job should run nightly to:\r 1. retrieve all active appeals assigned to reader users  this will need to be limited in the future as well when opened to 700 users \r 1. loop through the appeals\r 1. for each appeal, retrieve the list of documents for the case from efolder. \r     - this request triggers efolder to cache these documents from vbms into s3\r \r currently, the job has no way to tell if efolder has to make requests to vbms for the documents. \r \r this ticket may need a tech spec for how to implement the required changes to the efolder api to return a field that shows if an appeal has to be fetched from vbms or not
text: change retrievedocsforreader job to limit requests made by efolder to vbms. before efolder is enabled for reader, the retrievedocumentsforreader batch job needs to be adjusted to limit the number of requests made by efolder to vbms.\r \r the batch job should run nightly to:\r 1. retrieve all active appeals assigned to reader users  this will need to be limited in the future as well when opened to 700 users \r 1. loop through the appeals\r 1. for each appeal, retrieve the list of documents for the case from efolder. \r     - this request triggers efolder to cache these documents from vbms into s3\r \r currently, the job has no way to tell if efolder has to make requests to vbms for the documents. \r \r this ticket may need a tech spec for how to implement the required changes to the efolder api to return a field that shows if an appeal has to be fetched from vbms or not

title: parameter spread on import results in usage of undeclared variable
body: bug report\r in the current 7.0.0-beta.5, a combination of parameter spread and an imported variable causes usage of an undeclared variable as the first argument to  apply  due to a missing variable name substitution\r     input code\r    javascript\r import abc from 'abc';\r const arr =  1, 2, 3 ;\r abc.xyz ...arr ;\r    \r \r     babel configuration  .babelrc, package.json, cli command \r used repl. also tested locally with:\r    bash\r npx babel --preset=env index.js\r    \r    json\r {\r   \ presets\ :  \r      \r       \ env or @babel/preset-env, depending on beta version\ ,\r       {\r         \ usebuiltins\ : \ usage\ ,\r         \ targets\ : {\r           \ browsers\ :  \ > 2%\  \r         }\r       }\r      \r    ,\r   \ plugins\ :   \r }\r    \r \r     expected behavior\r something equivalent to this output from 6.26.0  value of the default import passed as first argument to  apply  \r https://babeljs.io/repl/ ?babili=false&browsers=&build=&builtins=false&code_lz=jywwdg9gtglgbaqweygm4dmorha5mlxabgcguia7az3gsijgf44btargbo4amlgzgc6pagdoahge8axgaor8ulacupia&debug=false&circlecirepo=&evaluate=false&linewrap=true&presets=es2015%2creact%2cstage-2&targets=&version=6.26.0\r \r     current behavior\r variable name not substituted => undeclared variable passed as first argument to  apply  => error\r https://babeljs.io/repl/build/5552 ?babili=false&browsers=&build=&builtins=false&code_lz=jywwdg9gtglgbaqweygm4dmorha5mlxabgcguia7az3gsijgf44btargbo4amlgzgc6pagdoahge8axgaor8ulacupia&debug=false&circlecirepo=&evaluate=true&linewrap=false&presets=es2015%2creact%2cstage-2&targets=&version=7.0.0-beta.5\r \r     your environment\r in addition to the repl, i've tested this with the cli.\r 7.0.0-beta.1 still works:  _abc2.default.xyz.apply _abc2.default, arr ; \r 7.0.0-beta.2 is broken:  _abc.default.xyz.apply abc, arr ;

title: infinite size checker doesn't see through impl trait, leading to rustc overflow
body: test case:\r \r    rust\r  ! feature conservative_impl_trait  \r \r trait quux {}\r \r fn foo   -> impl quux {\r     struct foo<t> t ;\r     impl<t> quux for foo<t> {}\r     foo bar   \r }\r \r fn bar   -> impl quux {\r     struct bar<t> t ;\r     impl<t> quux for bar<t> {}\r     bar foo   \r }\r \r // effectively:\r //     struct foo bar ;\r //     struct bar foo ;\r // should produce an error about infinite size\r \r fn main   { foo  ; }\r    \r \r output:\r \r    \r thread 'rustc' has overflowed its stack\r fatal runtime error: stack overflow\r    \r \r  rustc --version --verbose :\r \r    \r rustc 1.15.0-nightly  03bdaade2 2016-11-27 \r binary: rustc\r commit-hash: 03bdaade2a0c39118a5be927c667776a5de0d681\r commit-date: 2016-11-27\r host: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu\r release: 1.15.0-nightly\r llvm version: 3.9\r

title: give a convenient way to retrieve shadow variables created by exponentialmovingaverage.
body: at first i was under the impression, the variables returned by  tf.moving_average_variables    would be shadow variables created, but that does not seem to be the case  they didn't save/restore correctly for me . \r \r now that i'm reading the code, that seems to be the intention, though? shouldn't  this line  https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blame/f2bd0fc399606d14b55f3f7d732d013f32b33dd5/tensorflow/python/training/moving_averages.py l227  read \r \r     ops.add_to_collection ops.graphkeys.moving_average_variables, avg \r \r ? \r \r if not, it would be great to save the averages under some other key and make them easily retrievable for the saver in a similar way as  tf.moving_average_variables   , maybe as   tf.moving_average_shadow_variables   .
text: give a convenient way to retrieve shadow variables created by exponentialmovingaverage.. at first i was under the impression, the variables returned by  tf.moving_average_variables    would be shadow variables created, but that does not seem to be the case  they didn't save/restore correctly for me . \r \r now that i'm reading the code, that seems to be the intention, though? shouldn't  this line  https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blame/f2bd0fc399606d14b55f3f7d732d013f32b33dd5/tensorflow/python/training/moving_averages.py l227  read \r \r     ops.add_to_collection ops.graphkeys.moving_average_variables, avg \r \r ? \r \r if not, it would be great to save the averages under some other key and make them easily retrievable for the saver in a similar way as  tf.moving_average_variables   , maybe as   tf.moving_average_shadow_variables   .

title: jet40 install fails on 64 bits since the last winetricks release.
body: hello,\r \r since the last release and the usage of winetricks.log to check what has already been installed, the attempt to install mdac27 on 64bit arch now fails  which is good .\r however a side effect is that it's now impossible to install jet40 on 64bit arch.\r \r here's a simple  tested  patch that restores the functionnality.\r \r best regards and happy new year,\r \r vincent.\r \r    \r --- winetricks.orig\t2018-01-09 16:26:46.154067926 +0100\r +++ winetricks\t2018-01-09 16:27:50.982599748 +0100\r @@ -7835,7 +7835,11 @@\r  \r  load_jet40  \r  {\r -    w_call mdac27\r +    if   \ $w_arch\  = \ win64\    ; then\r +        w_call mdac28\r +    else\r +        w_call mdac27\r +    fi\r      w_call wsh57\r  \r        https://support.microsoft.com/kb/239114\r    \r \r
text: jet40 install fails on 64 bits since the last winetricks release.. hello,\r \r since the last release and the usage of winetricks.log to check what has already been installed, the attempt to install mdac27 on 64bit arch now fails  which is good .\r however a side effect is that it's now impossible to install jet40 on 64bit arch.\r \r here's a simple  tested  patch that restores the functionnality.\r \r best regards and happy new year,\r \r vincent.\r \r    \r --- winetricks.orig\t2018-01-09 16:26:46.154067926 +0100\r +++ winetricks\t2018-01-09 16:27:50.982599748 +0100\r @@ -7835,7 +7835,11 @@\r  \r  load_jet40  \r  {\r -    w_call mdac27\r +    if   \ $w_arch\  = \ win64\    ; then\r +        w_call mdac28\r +    else\r +        w_call mdac27\r +    fi\r      w_call wsh57\r  \r        https://support.microsoft.com/kb/239114\r    \r \r

title: memory leak -> capacity readout hangs
body: due to a memory leak in the nlg6 swrev string storage the heap memory is insuficient for the bms all query and the arduino uno will hang when reserving memory for the capacity values.\r \r quick fix is to disable the nlg6 test   set  nlg6test 0   in the ed_bmsdiag.h:\r    \r  define nlg6test 1               //!< test if the nlg6 fast charger is installed\r    \r
text: memory leak -> capacity readout hangs. due to a memory leak in the nlg6 swrev string storage the heap memory is insuficient for the bms all query and the arduino uno will hang when reserving memory for the capacity values.\r \r quick fix is to disable the nlg6 test   set  nlg6test 0   in the ed_bmsdiag.h:\r    \r  define nlg6test 1               //!< test if the nlg6 fast charger is installed\r    \r

title: mistake in documentation about creating-a-resource-with-post
body: the example provided for python-requests is faulty.  http://plonerestapi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/crud.html creating-a-resource-with-post \r instead of  requests.post 'http://localhost:8080/plone/folder', auth= 'admin', 'admin' , headers={'accept': 'application/json'}, params={'@type': 'document'}  \r it should be  requests.post 'http://localhost:8080/plone/folder', auth= 'admin', 'admin' , headers={'accept': 'application/json'}, json={'@type': 'document'}   \r  replace  params  with  json

title: variables scoped to environments that are part of a snapshot
body: issue  \r \r when you have a snapshotted variable as part of a release that has been scoped to an environment, when that environment is removed the variable remains in the snapshot however the _scoping_ is removed making the value global/un-scoped\r \r   replication  \r \r testing performing in 3.17.6\r \r create a testing environment  and add machines , project and lifecycle. create a variable that is scoped to the environment and deploy a release. remove the environment and note that the variable remains in the release  and is removed from the project , however the scoping of the variable has been removed.\r
text: variables scoped to environments that are part of a snapshot. issue  \r \r when you have a snapshotted variable as part of a release that has been scoped to an environment, when that environment is removed the variable remains in the snapshot however the _scoping_ is removed making the value global/un-scoped\r \r   replication  \r \r testing performing in 3.17.6\r \r create a testing environment  and add machines , project and lifecycle. create a variable that is scoped to the environment and deploy a release. remove the environment and note that the variable remains in the release  and is removed from the project , however the scoping of the variable has been removed.\r

title: glance_image can't set owner to non-member tenant
body: the  owner  parameter of the  glance_image  module only works when 'login_tenant_name' is the same. you can't set  login_tenant_name  to a tenant that you aren't a member because you get a permissions error.\r \r the glance command line tools and v2 api don't have this issue.

title: re-think access to calendar and overview
body: yaml   ticket imported from trac      right now, due to 62dbb65102b9e2812ccdf51ad210479663505c63, we are forcing authentication when people access the calendar for any category. this behavior is not only incorrect  as a category may be public , but inconsistent with the category overview too.  i suggest the following changes: - letting everyone access the calendar and the overview, and filtering out events that cannot be seen - using the same criterion on both sides - visibility?
text: re-think access to calendar and overview. yaml   ticket imported from trac      right now, due to 62dbb65102b9e2812ccdf51ad210479663505c63, we are forcing authentication when people access the calendar for any category. this behavior is not only incorrect  as a category may be public , but inconsistent with the category overview too.  i suggest the following changes: - letting everyone access the calendar and the overview, and filtering out events that cannot be seen - using the same criterion on both sides - visibility?

title: 'npm run test' should stop all started processes on failure
body: java backend remains running after failing frontend tests. that makes is cumbersome to retry tests because re-running them fails due to some local ports being busy.  steps to reproduce: - make sure nothing is running on the port 8080 - run  $ npm start -- -- false  - wait until it fails - boom, java is still running in the background, taking ports 8080 and 9001

title: fix getclassop mess
body: the  getclass   primop does type inspection in order to determine the which  java.lang.class  object to retrieve for jwts. this works great for fully instantiated functions  the common case of  getclass    but will give undesired results for polymorphic, uninlined/unspecialized functions. this is  bad  and will be a wart in the standard library if not fixed asap. \r \r the solution would be to:\r \r 1. add a new method to the  class  typeclass:\r \r    haskell\r instance class a where\r   ..\r   classidentifier :: jstring\r    \r and this will store the actual class name for a jwt that can be retrieved properly through the standard typeclass dispatch mechanism.\r \r 2. modify the deriving mechanism for  class  to fill in  classidentifier  by extracting the class annotation from the jwt. this step is crucial and will automatically fix existing code since the docs already recommend to use  deriving class .
text: fix getclassop mess. the  getclass   primop does type inspection in order to determine the which  java.lang.class  object to retrieve for jwts. this works great for fully instantiated functions  the common case of  getclass    but will give undesired results for polymorphic, uninlined/unspecialized functions. this is  bad  and will be a wart in the standard library if not fixed asap. \r \r the solution would be to:\r \r 1. add a new method to the  class  typeclass:\r \r    haskell\r instance class a where\r   ..\r   classidentifier :: jstring\r    \r and this will store the actual class name for a jwt that can be retrieved properly through the standard typeclass dispatch mechanism.\r \r 2. modify the deriving mechanism for  class  to fill in  classidentifier  by extracting the class annotation from the jwt. this step is crucial and will automatically fix existing code since the docs already recommend to use  deriving class .

title: tcpstream::peer_addr and local_addr fail on windows
body: i'm running into an issue where the same code behaves differently on windows and linux  big surprise :smile: . it's led one user to request changes to the api of ws-rs  see housleyjk/ws-rs 49 , but i believe the underlying issue has to do with  peer_addr  and  local_addr  on windows. this gist, derived from the simple tcp example in the docs and using only mio, shows that the same code runs perfectly on linux but fails on windows: https://gist.github.com/housleyjk/fa0ab22ef2264818c43d25ab2ed76ffb. if possible, i would like to avoid needing to provide a windows-specific api for obtaining the peer_addr in ws-rs, and i don't know how i can get the local_addr to work on windows.\r \r thank you for your consideration!

title: detection/error message of svg mime type looks to be incorrect
body: when scanning my website  https://www.rcfed.com  i get an error regarding the mime type of scg images:\r ! image  https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1877612/42865459-fb6e5dc8-8a69-11e8-8cfb-f70d13b30062.png \r \r i have investigated the issue and it looks like we are having an inconsistency. \r the error message states that the type is 'image/svg+xml' but should be 'image/svg+xml'.\r \r according to the w3c spec.  https://www.w3.org/tr/svg11/  the correct mime type is 'image/svg+xml': \r https://www.w3.org/tr/svg11/mimereg.html\r \r ! image  https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1877612/42865714-d2665164-8a6a-11e8-94a4-880f7692f3d0.png \r \r \r also 'w3c server settings'  https://www.w3.org/services/svg-server/  states 'images/svg+xml':\r ! image  https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1877612/42865611-7ba84e40-8a6a-11e8-8478-8800856af233.png \r \r if you have performed a scanning and follow the 'documentation link', then you end up at: https://sonarwhal.com/docs/user-guide/rules/rule-content-type/\r \r also here it is suggested to use mime type 'image/svg+xml':\r ! image  https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1877612/42865867-46b6e75e-8a6b-11e8-8906-906b29c33c7e.png \r \r

title: componentfailuretest.testplasmamanagerfailed test hangs frequently in travis in macos/python3 build.
body: the following test fails nearly every time in travis on the macos/python 3.5 build.\r \r    \r python test/component_failures_test.py componentfailuretest.testplasmamanagerfailed\r    \r \r note that the line https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/617bc4d2394aec607755a1a33f16271c38298f3f/test/component_failures_test.py l134 has a bug, it should just be  num_workers_per_scheduler . after making that change, i can reproduce the problem locally by running the test in a loop.\r \r investigating the bug a bit more, it appears that   somehow a task is assigned to a local scheduler that has been removed  . this shouldn't happen because the monitor should iterate through all of the tasks in the task table and clean them up. however, there may be a race condition.
text: componentfailuretest.testplasmamanagerfailed test hangs frequently in travis in macos/python3 build.. the following test fails nearly every time in travis on the macos/python 3.5 build.\r \r    \r python test/component_failures_test.py componentfailuretest.testplasmamanagerfailed\r    \r \r note that the line https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/617bc4d2394aec607755a1a33f16271c38298f3f/test/component_failures_test.py l134 has a bug, it should just be  num_workers_per_scheduler . after making that change, i can reproduce the problem locally by running the test in a loop.\r \r investigating the bug a bit more, it appears that   somehow a task is assigned to a local scheduler that has been removed  . this shouldn't happen because the monitor should iterate through all of the tasks in the task table and clean them up. however, there may be a race condition.

title: click.prompt combined with click.path skips path validation for prompt default
body: if using  click.prompt  with both  click.path  and a default value, any user input will be validated through  click.path , but the default value will not be.\r \r example:\r \r    python\r >>> import click\r >>> click.prompt 'path', type=click.path exists=true , default='/invalid/path' \r   invalid user input raises error\r path  /invalid/path : /some/other/invalid/path\r error: path \ /some/other/invalid/path\  does not exist.\r   invalid default value does not raise error\r path  /invalid/path :\r '/invalid/path'\r    \r \r tested with click 6.7, and python 2.7.13 and 3.6.1.
text: click.prompt combined with click.path skips path validation for prompt default. if using  click.prompt  with both  click.path  and a default value, any user input will be validated through  click.path , but the default value will not be.\r \r example:\r \r    python\r >>> import click\r >>> click.prompt 'path', type=click.path exists=true , default='/invalid/path' \r   invalid user input raises error\r path  /invalid/path : /some/other/invalid/path\r error: path \ /some/other/invalid/path\  does not exist.\r   invalid default value does not raise error\r path  /invalid/path :\r '/invalid/path'\r    \r \r tested with click 6.7, and python 2.7.13 and 3.6.1.

title: mode doesn't count players that didn't ready as alive
body: i'm not sure if you have noticed but if let's say 10 people join and 3 are ready when only the 3 die or go down the game mode acts as if everyone died and ends the game. the good news is i found the cause. \r \r short version: in rounds/sv_round the function player.getallnonspecs  ,\r you want \ if ply:isvalid   and ply:isready  \  replaced with \ if ply:isvalid   and ply:team   != team_spectator then\ \r \r long version: 7 players in the given example were not marked as ready. because of this they are not included in player.getallplaying   there for they are not included in player.getallplayingandalive  . as you know player.getallplayingandalive   is used to count how many players are alive and active and if the list is empty then the gamemode knows to end the game.

title: zenburn theme doesn't properly style new tabs
body: changing the theme to zenburn will correctly update all existing tabs, but new ones aren't styled correctly. they have a white background, with black text and the current line is highlighted in black, exactly the same color as the text, making it essentially unusable.\r \r if you go back into the settings, the theme switcher still says zenburn, but if you just hit okay  without changing anything  it will fix the problem, at least until more tabs are opened.\r \r <bountysource-plugin>\r ---\r want to back this issue?    post a bounty on it!  https://www.bountysource.com/issues/32415258-zenburn-theme-doesn-t-properly-style-new-tabs?utm_campaign=plugin&utm_content=tracker%2f346321&utm_medium=issues&utm_source=github    we accept bounties via  bountysource  https://www.bountysource.com/?utm_campaign=plugin&utm_content=tracker%2f346321&utm_medium=issues&utm_source=github .\r </bountysource-plugin>

title: geospatialcoordinateencoder \ altitude\  seems optional, but not really
body: i ran into difficulties generating anomaly likelihood values with a geospatialcoordinateencoder  gce , and i believe the root cause is that the  altitude  portion of the raw input vector.\r \r in all current examples of gce usage, the input vector is  created like this  https://github.com/numenta/nupic.geospatial/blob/master/model/geospatial_anomaly.py l136-l138 :\r \r    python\r modelinput = {\r     \ vector\ :  speed, longitude, latitude, altitude \r }\r    \r \r ... where  altitude  may be  none . this starts breaking when you use the anomaly likelihood post-process, because it introduces arrays with  none  values, and numpy complains when trying to  mean  the data:\r \r    \r traceback  most recent call last :\r   file \ /users/mtaylor/library/python/2.7/lib/python/site-packages/nupic/algorithms/anomaly_likelihood.py\ , line 208, in anomalyprobability\r     skiprecords=numskiprecords \r   file \ /users/mtaylor/library/python/2.7/lib/python/site-packages/nupic/algorithms/anomaly_likelihood.py\ , line 364, in estimateanomalylikelihoods\r     performlowerboundcheck=false \r   file \ /users/mtaylor/library/python/2.7/lib/python/site-packages/nupic/algorithms/anomaly_likelihood.py\ , line 578, in estimatenormal\r     \ mean\ : numpy.mean sampledata ,\r   file \ /system/library/frameworks/python.framework/versions/2.7/extras/lib/python/numpy/core/fromnumeric.py\ , line 2716, in mean\r     out=out, keepdims=keepdims \r   file \ /system/library/frameworks/python.framework/versions/2.7/extras/lib/python/numpy/core/_methods.py\ , line 62, in _mean\r     ret = um.add.reduce arr, axis=axis, dtype=dtype, out=out, keepdims=keepdims \r typeerror: unsupported operand type s  for +: 'float' and 'nonetype'\r    \r \r i tried using  numpy.nanmean  but that didn't help. another workaround is to use  0  for altitude, but this drastically increases processing time by adding another dimension to the input that doesn't really exist.\r \r if you look at how the gce  encodes input data  https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/src/nupic/encoders/geospatial_coordinate.py l81-l90 :\r \r    python\r altitude = none\r if len inputdata  == 4:\r    speed, longitude, latitude, altitude  = inputdata\r else:\r    speed, longitude, latitude  = inputdata\r coordinate = self.coordinateforposition longitude, latitude, altitude \r radius = self.radiusforspeed speed \r super geospatialcoordinateencoder, self .encodeintoarray \r   coordinate, radius , output \r    \r \r you can see that  altitude  is   optionally   included in the coordinate. however,  the description  https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/src/nupic/encoders/geospatial_coordinate.py l69-l71    always   returns the altitude:\r \r    python\r   def getdescription self :\r     \ \ \ see  nupic.encoders.base.encoder  for more information.\ \ \ \r     return   'speed', 0 ,  'longitude', 1 ,  'latitude', 2 ,  'altitude', 3  \r    \r \r i believe this is the root problem, but i'm not sure how to fix it. to get things running for my current project, i have just manually removed the   'altitude', 3   from the description array, and i can now run nupic without an  altitude  and post-process the anomaly likelihood properly.

title: question  shards sorting / shuffling
body: @srush @jianyuzhan @helson73 \r guys,\r i have a question for both the sharding preprocessing and the batching at training time.\r \r i observed that during an epoch the ppl starts quite high, keeps decreasing and after it's finished it starts again very high.\r i think it is due to the fact that the dataset may be sorted in some ways both during preprocess sharding, and also before batching in buckets.\r \r i think buckets of the same length must be shuffled between them so that we do not see this phenomenon of ppl starting high at the beg of each epoch.\r \r
text: question  shards sorting / shuffling. @srush @jianyuzhan @helson73 \r guys,\r i have a question for both the sharding preprocessing and the batching at training time.\r \r i observed that during an epoch the ppl starts quite high, keeps decreasing and after it's finished it starts again very high.\r i think it is due to the fact that the dataset may be sorted in some ways both during preprocess sharding, and also before batching in buckets.\r \r i think buckets of the same length must be shuffled between them so that we do not see this phenomenon of ppl starting high at the beg of each epoch.\r \r

title: hub remote add gives wrong url for github repositories
body: the default urls generated by hub for push do not work.\r \r when i add a new github repository as a remote using  hub remote add _user_ , a git protocol url is created. for example, the output of  hub remote -v on my repo is:\r \r    \r scici   git://github.com/scici/sci.git  fetch \r scici   git://github.com/scici/sci.git  push \r    \r from what i understand,  git:://  url will not work on push because the git protocol  lacks authentication  https://git-scm.com/book/en/v1/git-on-the-server-the-protocols the-git-protocol .  the url should be https. \r \r i'd be willing to fix this if someone could point me in the right direction.\r \r

title: syntax error when putting  def  in enum object after simple  case
body: this doesn't compile:\r    scala\r enum class ducks\r \r object ducks {\r   case dewey\r \r   def wooohoo: int = 1\r }\r    \r    scala\r --  e039  syntax error: enumo.scala:6:6 ------------------------------------\r 6 |  def woohoo: int = 1\r   |      ^^^^^^\r   |      ';' expected, but identifier found\r    \r this can be worked around by writing  case dewey;  or  case dewey extends ducks .

title: uow : idisposable
body: \r ! image  https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7582805/38421840-43e0b452-39b1-11e8-8d01-08c2c38d2c90.png \r \r \r dispose as a method name should be used exclusively to implement idisposable.dispose to prevent any confusion.\r \r it may be tempting to create a dispose method for other purposes, but doing so will result in confusion and likely lead to problems in production.\r \r noncompliant code example\r    \r public class garbagedisposal\r {\r   private int dispose    // noncompliant\r   {\r     // ...\r   }\r }\r    \r compliant solution\r    \r public class garbagedisposal : idisposable\r {\r   public void dispose  \r   {\r     // ...\r   }\r }\r or\r public class garbagedisposal\r {\r   private int grind  \r   {\r     // ...\r   }\r }\r    \r exceptions\r methods named dispose and invoked from the idisposable.dispose implementation are not reported.\r \r    \r public class garbagedisposal : idisposable\r {\r   protected virtual void dispose bool disposing \r   {\r     //...\r   }\r   public void dispose  \r   {\r     dispose true ;\r     gc.suppressfinalize this ;\r   }\r }\r
text: uow : idisposable. \r ! image  https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7582805/38421840-43e0b452-39b1-11e8-8d01-08c2c38d2c90.png \r \r \r dispose as a method name should be used exclusively to implement idisposable.dispose to prevent any confusion.\r \r it may be tempting to create a dispose method for other purposes, but doing so will result in confusion and likely lead to problems in production.\r \r noncompliant code example\r    \r public class garbagedisposal\r {\r   private int dispose    // noncompliant\r   {\r     // ...\r   }\r }\r    \r compliant solution\r    \r public class garbagedisposal : idisposable\r {\r   public void dispose  \r   {\r     // ...\r   }\r }\r or\r public class garbagedisposal\r {\r   private int grind  \r   {\r     // ...\r   }\r }\r    \r exceptions\r methods named dispose and invoked from the idisposable.dispose implementation are not reported.\r \r    \r public class garbagedisposal : idisposable\r {\r   protected virtual void dispose bool disposing \r   {\r     //...\r   }\r   public void dispose  \r   {\r     dispose true ;\r     gc.suppressfinalize this ;\r   }\r }\r

title: functionclauseerror in logger.debug_body/1
body: \r         functionclauseerror  no function clause matching in tesla.middleware.logger.debug_body/1\r          tesla  lib/tesla/middleware/logger.ex:207: tesla.middleware.logger.debug_body %googleapi.pubsub.v1.model.publishrequest\r    \r \r some libraries  like google-elixirs-apis  expect request to be a specific struct. \r the solution would be to add a catch-all clause to  debug_body/1  that would  inspect/1  the given argument.

title: kdump.service & network.service failing
body: is this a bug report or feature request?  :\r /kind bug\r  perhaps? \r \r   what happened  :\r i recently created a new tarmak cluster in response to support ticket. the customer referenced the failing kdump and network services.\r \r these were unrelated to the issue but they had caused confusion.\r \r on my nodes the kdump and network were failing as follows:\r \r | node    | kdump   | network |\r |---------|---------|---------|\r | master  |         | failing |\r | vault   | failing | failing |\r | worker  |         | failing |\r | etcd    |         | failing |\r | bastion | failing | failing |\r \r   what you expected to happen  :\r when a node is healthy, for it to have no failing systemd units so as not to distract from other issues.\r \r   how to reproduce it  as minimally and precisely as possible   :\r i have seen this on other, if not most clusters i've worked on before but have never looked into the cause.
