title: checkpoint never completes
body: is this a bug, feature request, or feedback?\r \r bug\r \r     what is the current behavior?\r \r while investigating  2504, i discovered that occasionally that the resilience demo scripting would hang waiting for output to be written to the  received.txt  file.  for example, with a single sequence window sender for 11 keys from sequence 1 to 500:\r \r    \r ubuntu@ip-10-0-121-64:~/wallaroo$ for i in  seq 0 10 ; do strings received.txt | sed 's/^.//' | grep key_$i | tail -1; done\r  key_0.traceid-1.tracewindow-1, 497,498,499,500  \r  key_10.traceid-1.tracewindow-1, 272,273,274,275  \r  key_2.traceid-1.tracewindow-1, 297,298,299,300  \r  key_3.traceid-1.tracewindow-1, 347,348,349,350  \r  key_4.traceid-1.tracewindow-1, 497,498,499,500  \r  key_5.traceid-1.tracewindow-1, 447,448,449,450  \r  key_6.traceid-1.tracewindow-1, 497,498,499,500  \r  key_7.traceid-1.tracewindow-1, 372,373,374,375  \r  key_8.traceid-1.tracewindow-1, 397,398,399,400  \r  key_9.traceid-1.tracewindow-1, 497,498,499,500  \r  key_10.traceid-1.tracewindow-1, 272,273,274,275  \r    \r \r it turns out that checkpoint  3 is not completing promptly; rather, it appears as if that checkpoint never completes.\r \r    \r 1538595268.714910 injecting barrier checkpointbarriertoken 3 \r 1538595268.714915 initiating barrier protocol for checkpointbarriertoken 3 .\r  ... \r 1538595269.003433 traceid-1 computing on key 'key_2' and value '500'\r 1538595269.003542 tcpsource connection closed\r 1538595273.210645 metrics outgoing connected\r 1538595863.514431 tcpsink connection closed\r    \r \r     what is the expected behavior?\r \r all 11 keys have output for sequence number 500 written to the  received.txt  file.\r \r     what os and version of wallaroo are you using? if you have a stacktrace and/or steps to reproduce the issue, that also helps a lot.\r \r ubuntu xenial 64-bit using wallaroo  master  at commit ae3112d1abb789271ca37416794d214117609468, using the sequence of commands:\r \r     cd demos/resilience-demo\r     sh ./10-setup-slf-repo.r=on.sh\r     ./verify.2worker.simple-restart.sh\r \r note that the  verify.2worker.simple-restart.sh  includes steps to stop & restart a wallaroo worker, but the logs at http://sendence-dev.s3.amazonaws.com/wallaroo/outstanding-problems/t101.a.tgz demonstrate the problem before the script's shutdown step.  this error happens approximately 10% of the time?\r \r cc: @jtfmumm \r

title: modifying aws iam user name deletes state
body: _this issue was originally opened by @alvin-huang as hashicorp/terraform 16646. it was migrated here as a result of the  provider split  https://www.hashicorp.com/blog/upcoming-provider-changes-in-terraform-0-10/ . the original body of the issue is below._\r \r <hr>\r \r     terraform version\r v0.10.8\r \r     terraform configuration files\r main.tf\r    hcl\r variable \ aws_region\  {}\r variable \ my_user\  {}\r \r provider \ aws\  {\r   region = \ ${var.aws_region}\ \r }\r \r resource \ aws_iam_user\  \ myuser\  {\r   name = \ ${var.my_user}\ \r   path = \ /\ \r }\r    \r \r createuser.tfvars\r    hcl\r aws_region  = \ us-east-1\ \r my_user = \ createuser\ \r    \r \r modifyuser.tfvars\r    hcl\r aws_region  = \ us-east-1\ \r my_user = \ modifyuser\ \r    \r \r     expected behavior\r the  createuser  aws user should be renamed to  modifyuser  and the state file should reflect this change.\r \r     debug\r https://gist.github.com/alvin-huang/e07b5e15b5837401b811db1acff84435\r \r     actual behavior\r the aws user does get renamed but the terraform state file is empty. \r \r     steps to reproduce\r 1.  terraform init \r 2.  terraform apply -var-file=createuser.tfvars  to create the  createuser  user\r 3.  terraform show  to show  createuser  is in created and in the state file\r 4.  terraform apply -var-file=modifyuser.tfvars  to change the name of  createuser  to  modifyuser \r 5.  terraform show  shows an empty state file\r \r     important factoids\r i see the same issue with a local state file and remote backend  s3 . \r \r     references\r terraform-providers/terraform-provider-aws 213\r hashicorp/terraform 3227\r \r if this should go in https://github.com/terraform-providers/terraform-provider-aws let me know and i can move it over there or hashibot will take care of it :

title: filtering works incorrectly
body: filtering by keyword in modules works incorrectly. values in columns are searched only by the start of the string. furthermore if two or more keywords are provided they are split and a separate _where_ clause is created for each keyword which are then joined together with and operator resulting in query which will never yield any results.
text: filtering works incorrectly. filtering by keyword in modules works incorrectly. values in columns are searched only by the start of the string. furthermore if two or more keywords are provided they are split and a separate _where_ clause is created for each keyword which are then joined together with and operator resulting in query which will never yield any results.

title: preview playback ends playing state, but doesn't stop playing when clicked

title: game_boy_advance not scraping titles with color in name.
body: using nintendo - game boy advance parent-clone  20170813-204027 .dat as base, via dat-o-matic p/c xml.  \r \r running the command  windows 10  \ vrec.exe main game_boy_advance \ nintendo - game boy advance parent-clone  20170813-204027 .dat\ \  results in an 8kb file, which contains only the entries that are not 'colored' in the listings on the wiki page.  i haven't seen this issue on the other consoles i have checked  nes/gbc/gb/snes , so it may be something to do with the formatting on that specific page.

title: player can smash wood barriers without holding a rock
body: when a player picks up a rock and smashes a wood barrier and thereafter dropping the rock, the player seems to be able to smash any other wood barrier without needing to carry a rock. \r \r a wood barrier should only be smashable when holding a rock.
text: player can smash wood barriers without holding a rock. when a player picks up a rock and smashes a wood barrier and thereafter dropping the rock, the player seems to be able to smash any other wood barrier without needing to carry a rock. \r \r a wood barrier should only be smashable when holding a rock.

title: opendkim not properly configured on ubuntu 16.04
body: impacted versions\r 1.10.4\r   distribution: debian / ubuntu / centos\r ubuntu\r   codename: jessie / trusty / centos 7 / ...\r xenial xerus\r   arch: 32 bits / 64 bits\r 64\r   database: postgresql / mysql\r postgresql \r   steps to reproduce\r install modoboa \r set new domain up and enable dkim \r send email\r \r   full trace using --debug option or current behaviour\r \r   expected behavior\r dkim signs outbound email \r   video/screenshot link  optional \r \r   fix / workaround\r this seems to be caused by /etc/default/opendkim only containing the default of \r socket=\ local:/var/run/opendkim/opendkim.sock\ \r \r postfix is configured to connect on port 12345\r \r adding this line to the  /etc/default/opendkim fixes the issue when you restart opendkim \r socket=\ inet:12345@127.0.0.1\ \r
text: opendkim not properly configured on ubuntu 16.04. impacted versions\r 1.10.4\r   distribution: debian / ubuntu / centos\r ubuntu\r   codename: jessie / trusty / centos 7 / ...\r xenial xerus\r   arch: 32 bits / 64 bits\r 64\r   database: postgresql / mysql\r postgresql \r   steps to reproduce\r install modoboa \r set new domain up and enable dkim \r send email\r \r   full trace using --debug option or current behaviour\r \r   expected behavior\r dkim signs outbound email \r   video/screenshot link  optional \r \r   fix / workaround\r this seems to be caused by /etc/default/opendkim only containing the default of \r socket=\ local:/var/run/opendkim/opendkim.sock\ \r \r postfix is configured to connect on port 12345\r \r adding this line to the  /etc/default/opendkim fixes the issue when you restart opendkim \r socket=\ inet:12345@127.0.0.1\ \r

title: invalid parameters crash cpp sp without warning
body: while inputdimensions= 4000,1,1  is ok.\r this might be something in swig  i'm testing it from python  from nupic.bindings.algorithms import spatialpooler    as in the c++ source is  vector<uint> inputdimensions \r \r edit:\r ok, figured further ..it is cause by some invalid init param settings, which do not print a warning but segfault! \r \r the code i was testing:\r    \r     coldim =  2048,1,1 \r     enc = rdse resolution=1 \r     enc = scalarencoder w=21, resolution=1, minval=0, maxval=10000 \r     indim =  enc.n,1,1 \r \r     print \ enc n=\ ,enc.n\r \r     sp = spatialpooler \r         inputdimensions=indim,\r         columndimensions=coldim,\r          potentialradius=3,\r          potentialpct=0.5,\r          globalinhibition=false,\r          localareadensity=-1.0,\r          numactivecolumnsperinharea=3,\r          stimulusthreshold=1,\r          synperminactivedec=0.01,\r          synpermactiveinc=0.1,\r          synpermconnected=0.10,\r          minpctoverlapdutycycle=0.1,\r          minpctactivedutycycle=0.1,\r          dutycycleperiod=10,\r          maxboost=10.0,\r         seed=getseed  ,\r         spverbosity=0 \r    \r \r commenting out \ fixed\  the problem, without it, you get the crash.

title: bootstrap: adjust grid on small browser
body: starting point: v2.html\r link: https://naturalintelligence.github.io/imglab/v2.html\r \r on top right side there are 3 buttons: plugins, keyboard, donate. when the browser window is < 1412px. they are not properly aligned. \r \r all the elements in a menu should be aligned for different screen size.

title: cannot pull changes after sync_gateway goes offline, then online
body: i am testing todolite android\r \r 1. i create a list and share it between 2 devices\r 2. i add a few task as see that they are shared correctly\r 3. i take the db offline via :4985/db/_offline\r    \r curl -x post http://localhost:4985/todos/_offline\r    \r 4. i add a few tasks on one client\r 5. i take the db online via :4985/db/_online\r    \r curl -x post http://localhost:4985/todos/_online\r    \r \r expected: i would expect the offline tasks to get pushed to sync_gateway and pulled to the other device.\r \r actual: offline tasks do not replicate to the other device.
text: cannot pull changes after sync_gateway goes offline, then online. i am testing todolite android\r \r 1. i create a list and share it between 2 devices\r 2. i add a few task as see that they are shared correctly\r 3. i take the db offline via :4985/db/_offline\r    \r curl -x post http://localhost:4985/todos/_offline\r    \r 4. i add a few tasks on one client\r 5. i take the db online via :4985/db/_online\r    \r curl -x post http://localhost:4985/todos/_online\r    \r \r expected: i would expect the offline tasks to get pushed to sync_gateway and pulled to the other device.\r \r actual: offline tasks do not replicate to the other device.

title: global services try to deploy on host that never activate
body: rancher versions:   6/14\r \r   steps to reproduce:  \r 1.  create a host that will fail to activate\r 2.  go to containers and look at the global system scaling groups\r \r   results:    all global scaling groups are trying to add a container to the host that errored.  it should not try to deploy to failed hosts.\r

title: autoscaling destroy all previous container on first action
body: using docker 17.06 very first action performed by orbiter will destroy all previous running containers instead scaling up or down.\r    \r server:\r  version:      17.06.0-ce\r  api version:  1.30  minimum version 1.12 \r  go version:   go1.8.3\r  git commit:   02c1d87\r  built:        fri jun 23 21:21:56 2017\r  os/arch:      linux/amd64\r  experimental: false\r    \r \r   the effect:   when docker first receive a request with old api signature will apply an operation of \ service update\  with new replica value. this actually will scale up/down as requested   and   will restart all previous running containers with the update policy defined.\r in case there was only one previous container running this lead to a service unavailability during restarts\r \r   the cause:    api changes from github.com/docker/docker 1.13.1 used by orbiter and the latest used by docker 17.06. latest version has a different signature on method _serviceinspectwithraw_    line 17 here  https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/361a473121f2cd4e688a3bb71fbeff00aaf674d7/client/service_inspect.go l17    adding new parameter of type _types.serviceinspectoptions_    godoc  https://godoc.org/github.com/moby/moby/client client.serviceinspectwithraw   \r \r solution proposed is to update docker client lib and change call to _serviceinspectwithraw_ method.\r @gianarb  i'm preparing a pull request but this changed could potentially impact other aspects or functionalities.\r
text: autoscaling destroy all previous container on first action. using docker 17.06 very first action performed by orbiter will destroy all previous running containers instead scaling up or down.\r    \r server:\r  version:      17.06.0-ce\r  api version:  1.30  minimum version 1.12 \r  go version:   go1.8.3\r  git commit:   02c1d87\r  built:        fri jun 23 21:21:56 2017\r  os/arch:      linux/amd64\r  experimental: false\r    \r \r   the effect:   when docker first receive a request with old api signature will apply an operation of \ service update\  with new replica value. this actually will scale up/down as requested   and   will restart all previous running containers with the update policy defined.\r in case there was only one previous container running this lead to a service unavailability during restarts\r \r   the cause:    api changes from github.com/docker/docker 1.13.1 used by orbiter and the latest used by docker 17.06. latest version has a different signature on method _serviceinspectwithraw_    line 17 here  https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/361a473121f2cd4e688a3bb71fbeff00aaf674d7/client/service_inspect.go l17    adding new parameter of type _types.serviceinspectoptions_    godoc  https://godoc.org/github.com/moby/moby/client client.serviceinspectwithraw   \r \r solution proposed is to update docker client lib and change call to _serviceinspectwithraw_ method.\r @gianarb  i'm preparing a pull request but this changed could potentially impact other aspects or functionalities.\r

title: libffi cannot handle double3, double4, scnvector3 function parameters
body: it appears that double3 and double4 vectors are not passed the same way  as other vectors to functions. while float2, float3, float4 and double2 vectors are being found in the xmm registers when the callee function executes when we pass double3 and double4 xmm0 and xmm1 contain invalid values.\r \r   update:   parameters of type scnvector3 are not passed properly as well.   the rest of the scnvectors should also be tested as well as the existing  in testrunner  structs as parameters.

title: glsl 4.50: layout qualifier table is missing  offset  when applied to atomic counters.
body: chapter 4.4 in glsl 4.50  revision 6  is missing an entry in the layout qualifier table. atomic counters can use the  offset  qualifier  4.4.6.1 , but it is not listed in the table as being valid.
text: glsl 4.50: layout qualifier table is missing  offset  when applied to atomic counters.. chapter 4.4 in glsl 4.50  revision 6  is missing an entry in the layout qualifier table. atomic counters can use the  offset  qualifier  4.4.6.1 , but it is not listed in the table as being valid.

title: vkphysicaldevicelimits has non-compliant value
body: the input_attachment api sample works fine on my macbook afaict  radeon pro 560 , but vulkaninfo reports a value of 0 for  maxperstagedescriptorinputattachments .\r \r if 0 were the actual limit, then the test would fail to create a valid descriptor set layout. this causes validation errors.\r \r this value must be at least 4 on compliant vulkan implementations, and i don't see a note about this in the   known moltenvk limitations   section.

title: add epel distros to tarantool.repo  centos/amazon linux
body: most tarantool packages for centos on tarantool.org/dist depend on packages in epel repository. yet enabling tarantol upstream on centos, using the instructions on our web site, does not enable epel. this leads to installation failures if epel was not enabled before. either mirror all relevant packages  libyaml, python-msgpack, perl-anyevent, etc, etc, etc  in our repo, or change download instructions to add epel as upstream  preferrable with a single copy and paste .

title: nightly build:  windows z88dk-install binary non-functional
body: z88dk-install.exe as produced by the nightly build is not working on windows.  i suspect it's this bit in z88dk-install.c:\r \r    \r  ifdef win32\r  include \ dirent.h\ \r  ifndef strcasecmp\r  define strcasecmp a,b  stricmp a,b \r  endif\r  else\r  include <dirent.h>\r  endif\r    \r \r the local dirent.h is an equivalent of dirent.h for windows as mentioned elsewhere and likely does not work with the cross-compiler.  i'm guessing the cross-compiler has a proper <dirent.h> to use instead, in which case a special test in   ifdef win32  must be added.  if not, i'll have to revert to windows only code again.\r

title: rebalance the game
body: my current thoughts on rebalancing:\r \r     the player    \r \r - decrease the initial amount of bullets you get. you should start with a really low amount, so each new heart you get from an enemy feels valuable, and feel like you're getting stronger, even with just this short demo level.\r - until such a time we can implement the leveling-up system, make the heart-bar extend to fit your max-heartage. this means every heart you pick up is added to a max-heart variable, and every time you lose one forever  by getting hit by an enemy , the max-heart variable is decremented. the heart-bar class will need to be updated to read from the player's max-heart variable, instead of from a constant.\r \r     the baddies    \r \r - increase the range of their bullets.\r - decrease the firing rate of their bullets to, like, 500 ms.\r - make their bullets bigger, so you can really see them.\r - add more baddies.

title: cannot using dataprovider to get data which set by smart contract
body: in smart contract, if we set data to state cache, it's not a problem to get data via executing transactions, but we cannot get data by constructing a dataprovider, it seems that there's something wrong with the data pointer.\r but if we set data directly to database, then there's no problem at all.\r maybe this is caused by pipeline set mechanism, still finding the root cause or considering refactor this part soon.

title: ict triggered builds not guaranteed to use correct image
body: this change: https://github.com/openshift/origin/commit/c431087f0321a0da31f6c975d0421e67bde2e94e diff-717854e27d42bef56a972e64d148b529l78  though line 90 means that when we have a build triggered by an ict, we are no longer guaranteed to build using the image that caused the ict to fire, we're using the current image associated with the tag when the build finally runs  because that's when the reference gets resolved now, instead of it being set at the time the event occurs .
text: ict triggered builds not guaranteed to use correct image. this change: https://github.com/openshift/origin/commit/c431087f0321a0da31f6c975d0421e67bde2e94e diff-717854e27d42bef56a972e64d148b529l78  though line 90 means that when we have a build triggered by an ict, we are no longer guaranteed to build using the image that caused the ict to fire, we're using the current image associated with the tag when the build finally runs  because that's when the reference gets resolved now, instead of it being set at the time the event occurs .

title: viewer: vocab table values aren't loading in dropdowns when editing metadata
body: @svoinea found that when editing the metadata for an image on the viewer app, the dropdowns aren't loading the domain values from the vocab tables.\r \r this might be because the first dropdown is unable to find table  image_grade_code , which seems to no longer exist in the  rbk  schema. the app throws an error, causing the rest of the code that populates the other dropdowns in to not run in  viewer.app.js .
text: viewer: vocab table values aren't loading in dropdowns when editing metadata. @svoinea found that when editing the metadata for an image on the viewer app, the dropdowns aren't loading the domain values from the vocab tables.\r \r this might be because the first dropdown is unable to find table  image_grade_code , which seems to no longer exist in the  rbk  schema. the app throws an error, causing the rest of the code that populates the other dropdowns in to not run in  viewer.app.js .

title: wrong phrase in the http.response.phrase field
body: - version: packetbeat 5.5.0\r - os: red hat enterprise linux server release 6.7  santiago   linux 2.6.32-504.8.1.el6.x86_64  1 smp wed jan 28 21:11:36 utc 2015 \r - steps to reproduce: parse an http 500 or http 404 response with packetbeat and inspect the http.response.phrase field in the produced event.\r \r it appears that packetbeat 5.5.0  the http module  only gets the last word from http status line when searching for the response phrase  i.e.  http/1.1 404 not found  turns into \ found\  in the  http.response.phrase  field . more details are available in  this forum post  https://discuss.elastic.co/t/wrong-phrase-in-the-http-response-phrase-field/95182 .\r \r examples of http response headers and status lines that produce wrong http.response.phrase values:\r    \r http/1.1 404 not found\r server: apache-coyote/1.1\r content-type: text/html;charset=utf-8\r content-length: 949\r date: mon, 31 jul 2017 11:31:53 gmt\r \r http/1.1 500 internal server error\r server: apache-coyote/1.1\r content-type: text/html;charset=utf-8\r content-length: 3739\r date: mon, 31 jul 2017 11:33:28 gmt\r connection: close\r

title: incorect font size in reference
body: tests a.1.0, a.2.0, a.3.0, a.4.0, b.1.0, b.2.0, c.2.0 have their font size as 8 but the image depiction is closer to 11.\r \r this is caused by a misinterpretation of the serialization of these test with font size 8points instead of 8px.\r \r test case c.1.0 has a font size of 16.35 but the image depiction is closer to 11 as well.

title: 945gc  fsb800 is unstable
body: the ga-945gcm-s2l is very unstable with an fsb800 chip, such as the e4600. memtest reports hundreds of errors and running  stress-ng --cpu 2 --vm 2  results in many kernel oopses. some dimm's even crash immediately after logging in to a composited desktop  artifacts, hard lockup .\r \r using the same board and the same dimm's with an fsb1066 chip, such as the e7500, everything is fine and stable for hours.\r \r some dimm's do work on fsb800 without issue though. but very few do.
text: 945gc  fsb800 is unstable. the ga-945gcm-s2l is very unstable with an fsb800 chip, such as the e4600. memtest reports hundreds of errors and running  stress-ng --cpu 2 --vm 2  results in many kernel oopses. some dimm's even crash immediately after logging in to a composited desktop  artifacts, hard lockup .\r \r using the same board and the same dimm's with an fsb1066 chip, such as the e7500, everything is fine and stable for hours.\r \r some dimm's do work on fsb800 without issue though. but very few do.

title: resizing a dynamicfont by code can corrupt it

title: poor mobile performance introduced between 1.9 and 1.10
body: i updated our earthviewer application from cesium 1.6 to cesium 1.12 yesterday and noticed that performance on mobile devices took a giant hit.  i tested on a nexus 7, and galaxy s tab and two ipads and noticed the same thing.   i don't notice a difference on my desktop so i'm not sure if it's a mobile only thing or if my desktop is so fast that the difference is negligible.  the performance hit took place between the 1.9 and 1.10 release  and is still present in the 1.12 release .  i've put an example up that shows the issue.  try hitting this with a mobile device.    example using 1.9, will be fast and responsive: https://s3.amazonaws.com/jason.beverage.uploads/cesium-performance-test/cesium-1.9.html  same example but with 1.10, will be very slow to rotate the globe: https://s3.amazonaws.com/jason.beverage.uploads/cesium-performance-test/cesium-1.10.html

title: outofmemoryerror for orthogonal init  keras 2

title: leaking pinned byte arrays of size 1 in http streaming scenario
body: we're leaking pinned gchandles on byte arrays each has just 1 element:\r ~~~\r 00000070ce2f8ac8 pinned      00000072d2e86b48       25                  system.byte  \r 00000070ce2f8ad0 pinned      0000007352e14c28       25                  system.byte  \r 00000070ce2f8ad8 pinned      0000007452853fb0       25                  system.byte  \r 00000070ce2f8ae0 pinned      00000073d12b8b88       25                  system.byte  \r 00000070ce2f8ae8 pinned      00000072522ede08       25                  system.byte  \r ~~~\r and they look like this:\r ~~~\r 0:021> !dumparray -details 000000725396b7b0\r name:        system.byte  \r methodtable: 00007ffd4ae4e0f8\r eeclass:     00007ffd4a5f6a28\r size:        25 0x19  bytes\r array:       rank 1, number of elements 1, type byte\r element methodtable: 00007ffd4ae68958\r  0  000000725396b7c0\r     name:        system.byte\r     methodtable: 00007ffd4ae68958\r     eeclass:     00007ffd4a5fc550\r     size:        24 0x18  bytes\r     file:        c:\\users\\kkhurin\\.dnx\\runtimes\\dnx-coreclr-win-x64.1.0.0-rc2-16551\\bin\\mscorlib.ni.dll\r     fields:\r                       mt    field   offset                 type vt     attr            value name\r         00007ffd4ae68958  40002bb        0              system.byte      1     instance                    0     m_value\r ~~~\r here is how to repro it with \ sharedpoolsofwcfobjects\  stress test:\r ~~~\r dnx  --configuration retail run .  program2run:stress stressrunduration:2  binding:http async:true test:streaming\r ~~~\r
text: leaking pinned byte arrays of size 1 in http streaming scenario. we're leaking pinned gchandles on byte arrays each has just 1 element:\r ~~~\r 00000070ce2f8ac8 pinned      00000072d2e86b48       25                  system.byte  \r 00000070ce2f8ad0 pinned      0000007352e14c28       25                  system.byte  \r 00000070ce2f8ad8 pinned      0000007452853fb0       25                  system.byte  \r 00000070ce2f8ae0 pinned      00000073d12b8b88       25                  system.byte  \r 00000070ce2f8ae8 pinned      00000072522ede08       25                  system.byte  \r ~~~\r and they look like this:\r ~~~\r 0:021> !dumparray -details 000000725396b7b0\r name:        system.byte  \r methodtable: 00007ffd4ae4e0f8\r eeclass:     00007ffd4a5f6a28\r size:        25 0x19  bytes\r array:       rank 1, number of elements 1, type byte\r element methodtable: 00007ffd4ae68958\r  0  000000725396b7c0\r     name:        system.byte\r     methodtable: 00007ffd4ae68958\r     eeclass:     00007ffd4a5fc550\r     size:        24 0x18  bytes\r     file:        c:\\users\\kkhurin\\.dnx\\runtimes\\dnx-coreclr-win-x64.1.0.0-rc2-16551\\bin\\mscorlib.ni.dll\r     fields:\r                       mt    field   offset                 type vt     attr            value name\r         00007ffd4ae68958  40002bb        0              system.byte      1     instance                    0     m_value\r ~~~\r here is how to repro it with \ sharedpoolsofwcfobjects\  stress test:\r ~~~\r dnx  --configuration retail run .  program2run:stress stressrunduration:2  binding:http async:true test:streaming\r ~~~\r

title: minor /setworth message error
body: i've noticed when you set the value of an item for /sell it does set the right price however the price variable in the message is in the wrong order. \r \r for example if i set the price of a rotten flesh to $0.05 it will set the correct price however it will return the message  \ the item rotten flesh is now available to sell to the server for 0.00 dollars  it was previously available for 0.05 dollars \   http://i.imgur.com/ga6ommf.png \r \r the current price displays the old price, and the previous displays the current price.

title: windows timezone preprocessor doesn't detect timezone correctly

title: need better way calculate time to wait for scans
body: issue type\r - bug _ when camayoc is not behaving correctly \r \r   description of issue\r currently we are having some issue on jenkins because it is running a lot of scans and we are not waiting long enough for some of them.\r \r   completion checklist\r to close this issue, the following items must be satisfied:\r -     check out what scans are taking so long on jenkins\r -     figure out a way to more intelligently decide on how long to wait. maybe could even add parameter in the config file. i think the ones with extended product searches and satellite are probably our culprits.

title: given when craft fail with chance ratio, the object at target allows user to do again
body: description:\r when craft fails with the chance ratio, the object at target allows user to do the same process again, which means user is told \ couldn't find anything\  but still allows user to keep on trying until they get something. to keep people on their feet, might as well allow block user from scavenging same object even if it fails initial check with chance.\r env:\r ver: 2017/10/16\r env: exile 1.0.3 / chernarus redux v0.41\r \r steps to repro:\r 1. make given craft ratio to something very low  example: water \r 2. start server\r 3. find water barrel or pump in server\r 4. start craft water\r 5. observe fail\r 6. try again\r \r expected results:\r the object at target shall not allow user to try to craft again, as they have already tried and are told they couldn't\r \r actual results:\r the object at question keeps allowing user to try to craft

title: bug  api inputs not validated on datasource create
body: my grafana version is  4.4.3 .\r \r this...\r    \r post /api/datasources http/1.1\r host: localhost:3000\r connection: keep-alive\r accept-encoding: gzip, deflate\r accept:  / \r user-agent: python-requests/2.12.4\r content-type: application/json\r content-length: 373\r authorization: basic xxxxxxx\r \r {\ name\ : \ local_cloudwatch\ , \ basicauthpassword\ : null, \ database\ : null, \ url\ : \ \ , \ basicauth\ : null, \ jsondata\ : \ {authtype: \\\ arn\\\ , timefield: \\\ @timestamp\\\ , defaultregion: \\\ us-west-1\\\ }\ , \ access\ : \ proxy\ , \ typelogourl\ : null, \ user\ : null, \ tlsauth\ : null, \ basicauthuser\ : null, \ password\ : null, \ type\ : \ cloudwatch\ , \ isdefault\ : true, \ withcredentials\ : null}\r \r http/1.1 200 ok\r content-type: application/json; charset=utf-8\r date: sat, 26 aug 2017 04:32:05 gmt\r content-length: 63\r \r {\ id\ :2,\ message\ :\ datasource added\ ,\ name\ :\ local_cloudwatch\ }\r    \r ...should not have succeeded.\r really, there are two bugs, here.\r \r the first bug is that clearly no validation happens of the json received from the api call; it just gets put somewhere verbatim. that's bad. from a usability perspective, that's bad, because it doesn't let you know when something's malformed.\r \r the second bug is that, after making this call, if you try to look at the datasource list in the ui, nothing loads and you get an unhappy orange box telling you a problem occurred. which takes this from being a concerning possible security flaw to a dos vulnerability that's easy to trigger.\r \r incidentally, this pathological edge case wasn't manually crafted- i was using the  grafana4_datasource  salt state. that module has a bug where it sends all those nulls. i'll be opening a pr for that soon, but in that's a different bug in a different project.\r \r otbs, the datasource still works for creating dashboards.

title: rigid_gripper demo cannot run for less than 0.1 simulation seconds resulting in ci timeout
body: the  rigid_gripper  demo timed out in the following ci run:\r \r https://drake-jenkins.csail.mit.edu/view/continuous%20production/job/linux-gcc-continuous-ros-debug/1368/\r \r here is the error:\r \r    \r 20:08:32 278/284 test  257: rigid_gripper ......................................   timeout  60.55 sec\r    \r \r skimming through  drake-distro/drake/examples/contact_model/rigid_gripper.cc , i believe the simulation cannot be executed for less than  kprintperiod , which is 0.1 simulation seconds. this executable is supposed to run as a unit test for only 0.01 simulation seconds according to  drake-distro/drake/examples/contact_model/cmakelists.txt .

title: electron  firebase.auth   doesn't return displayname
body: after logging in to the application,  onauthstatechanged   in appcomponent  the current user is updated with the new information. this information does not contain valid data for displayname which is needed for the userlist/.\r \r so for the time being, electron will use user.email instead of user.name for anything regarding user specific info  info that doesn't use user.id . this may effect notifications, displaying lasteditedby, and displaying owner.
text: electron  firebase.auth   doesn't return displayname. after logging in to the application,  onauthstatechanged   in appcomponent  the current user is updated with the new information. this information does not contain valid data for displayname which is needed for the userlist/.\r \r so for the time being, electron will use user.email instead of user.name for anything regarding user specific info  info that doesn't use user.id . this may effect notifications, displaying lasteditedby, and displaying owner.

title: hosting multiple service workers on the same origin deletes the cache from other subdirectories
body: library affected  :\r workbox-precaching\r \r   browser & platform  :\r all browsers\r \r   issue or feature request description  :\r i have multiple web apps hosted on the same domain that each have a service worker set up to allow the use of the app offline. for example, https://benlubar.github.io/cmv2mp4/ and https://benlubar.github.io/df-ai/plan-editor/.\r \r setting a different cache name prefix on each only affects the name of the cache, which already contains the url of the directory where the service worker is hosted. the indexeddb is always named  workbox-precaching  and contains no information about which subdirectory the service worker is hosted in.
text: hosting multiple service workers on the same origin deletes the cache from other subdirectories. library affected  :\r workbox-precaching\r \r   browser & platform  :\r all browsers\r \r   issue or feature request description  :\r i have multiple web apps hosted on the same domain that each have a service worker set up to allow the use of the app offline. for example, https://benlubar.github.io/cmv2mp4/ and https://benlubar.github.io/df-ai/plan-editor/.\r \r setting a different cache name prefix on each only affects the name of the cache, which already contains the url of the directory where the service worker is hosted. the indexeddb is always named  workbox-precaching  and contains no information about which subdirectory the service worker is hosted in.

title: file manager shows  object object  instead of errors
body: when pufferd returns an error for the file manager, or some error occurs while talking to the daemon, instead of a message being printed that explains what happened, a  object object  is shown, which has no useful information.\r \r we should display the error correctly.
text: file manager shows  object object  instead of errors. when pufferd returns an error for the file manager, or some error occurs while talking to the daemon, instead of a message being printed that explains what happened, a  object object  is shown, which has no useful information.\r \r we should display the error correctly.

title: 'time.units' being dropped if differs across files being loaded
body: the netcdf data for a cmip5 simulation i am trying to use is split into two netcdf files along the time dimension, as is common.  but, annoyingly, they have encoded a different  units  attribute for each file.  i think, as a result, the attribute is being dropped when  open_mfdataset  is called, based on this partial traceback:\r \r    \r   file \ /home/spencer.hill/py/aospy/aospy/data_loader.py\ , line 246, in load_variable\r     ds, min_year, max_year = _prep_time_data ds \r   file \ /home/spencer.hill/py/aospy/aospy/data_loader.py\ , line 160, in _prep_time_data\r     ds, min_year, max_year = times.numpy_datetime_workaround_encode_cf ds \r   file \ /home/spencer.hill/py/aospy/aospy/utils/times.py\ , line 272, in numpy_datetime_workaround_encode_cf\r     units = time.attrs 'units' \r keyerror: 'units'\r    \r \r the place where the attribute is being dropped is in the  xr.open_mfdataset  call in  _load_data_from_disk  https://github.com/spencerahill/aospy/blob/develop/aospy/data_loader.py l173 .  so it would appear we need to add some logic that checks that the time units attrs are the same across all netcdf files to be loaded.  if not, we'll need to somehow force them to have the same units.  or something else?  @spencerkclark, what do you think?\r \r here's an  ncdump -h  for each:\r \r    \r $ ncdump -h pr_amon_ccsm4_amip4k_r1i1p1_197901-200512.nc\r netcdf pr_amon_ccsm4_amip4k_r1i1p1_197901-200512 {\r dimensions:\r \ttime = unlimited ; //  324 currently \r \tlat = 192 ;\r \tlon = 288 ;\r \tbnds = 2 ;\r variables:\r \tdouble time time  ;\r \t\ttime:bounds = \ time_bnds\  ;\r \t\ttime:units = \ days since 1979-01-01 00:00:00\  ;\r \t\ttime:calendar = \ noleap\  ;\r \t\ttime:axis = \ t\  ;\r \t\ttime:long_name = \ time\  ;\r \t\ttime:standard_name = \ time\  ;\r ...\r    \r and\r    \r $ ncdump -h pr_amon_ccsm4_amip4k_r1i1p1_200601-200812.nc\r netcdf pr_amon_ccsm4_amip4k_r1i1p1_200601-200812 {\r dimensions:\r \ttime = unlimited ; //  36 currently \r \tlat = 192 ;\r \tlon = 288 ;\r \tbnds = 2 ;\r variables:\r \tdouble time time  ;\r \t\ttime:bounds = \ time_bnds\  ;\r \t\ttime:units = \ days since 2004-01-01 00:00:00\  ;\r \t\ttime:calendar = \ noleap\  ;\r \t\ttime:axis = \ t\  ;\r \t\ttime:long_name = \ time\  ;\r \t\ttime:standard_name = \ time\  ;\r ...\r    \r \r for those w/ gfdl access, the files in question are  \r \r    \r /archive/pcmdi/repo/cmip5/output/ncar/ccsm4/amip4k/mon/atmos/amon/r1i1p1/v20120711/pr/pr_amon_ccsm4_amip4k_r1i1p1_197901-200512.nc\r /archive/pcmdi/repo/cmip5/output/ncar/ccsm4/amip4k/mon/atmos/amon/r1i1p1/v20120711/pr/pr_amon_ccsm4_amip4k_r1i1p1_200601-200812.nc\r

title: flowadapters splits the org.reactivestreams package
body: it is impossible to consume the reactive streams flow adapters in jdk9 as a module since it splits the  org.reactivestreams  package across itself and the  org.reactivestreams  module  which it depends on . here's an example error message from javac when you try to use it:\r \r    \r error: module org.reactivestreams.tck reads package org.reactivestreams from both org.reactivestreams.flowadapters and org.reactivestreams\r    \r \r jdk9 does not allow packages split between modules.  flowadapters  must be moved to its own package if its to be in its own artifact.

title: objects should not call  eventbus.register this   in constructors
body: problem\r registering an object to the event bus inside of a constructor call can result in a subscriber being called prior to all of the fields in the class being fully constructed. this can lead to null pointer exceptions for fields that are final and should have been initialized in the classes constructor.\r \r  relavent gitter discussion  https://gitter.im/wpiroboticsprojects/grip?at=564baf1609446b3b7461702a  discovery of problem and discussion of how to fix it.\r \r  guava google groups discussion with dev  https://groups.google.com/forum/ !topic/guava-discuss/4zjhhpmmvkq \r \r >    don't publish the \ this\  reference during construction\r one of the mistakes that can introduce a data race into your class is to expose the this reference to another thread before the constructor has completed. sometimes the reference is explicit, such as directly storing this in a static field or collection, but other times it can be implicit, such as when you publish a reference to an instance of a non-static inner class in a constructor. constructors are not ordinary methods -- they have special semantics for initialization safety. an object is assumed to be in a predictable, consistent state after the constructor has completed, and publishing a reference to an incompletely constructed object is dangerous. listing 2 shows an example of introducing this sort of race condition into a constructor. it may look harmless, but it contains the seeds of serious concurrency problems.\r > \\- brian goetz  http://www.ibm.com/developerworks/library/j-jtp0618/ \r \r   resolution\r this can be resolved by ensuring that constructors never call a register method\r
text: objects should not call  eventbus.register this   in constructors. problem\r registering an object to the event bus inside of a constructor call can result in a subscriber being called prior to all of the fields in the class being fully constructed. this can lead to null pointer exceptions for fields that are final and should have been initialized in the classes constructor.\r \r  relavent gitter discussion  https://gitter.im/wpiroboticsprojects/grip?at=564baf1609446b3b7461702a  discovery of problem and discussion of how to fix it.\r \r  guava google groups discussion with dev  https://groups.google.com/forum/ !topic/guava-discuss/4zjhhpmmvkq \r \r >    don't publish the \ this\  reference during construction\r one of the mistakes that can introduce a data race into your class is to expose the this reference to another thread before the constructor has completed. sometimes the reference is explicit, such as directly storing this in a static field or collection, but other times it can be implicit, such as when you publish a reference to an instance of a non-static inner class in a constructor. constructors are not ordinary methods -- they have special semantics for initialization safety. an object is assumed to be in a predictable, consistent state after the constructor has completed, and publishing a reference to an incompletely constructed object is dangerous. listing 2 shows an example of introducing this sort of race condition into a constructor. it may look harmless, but it contains the seeds of serious concurrency problems.\r > \\- brian goetz  http://www.ibm.com/developerworks/library/j-jtp0618/ \r \r   resolution\r this can be resolved by ensuring that constructors never call a register method\r

title: fix incorrect foreign key for channelgroup_id in teamspeak_channel_group_permission
body: the following foreign key is currently set for table  teamspeak_channel_group_permission :  $table->foreign 'channelgroup_id' ->references 'id' ->on 'teamspeak_group' ->ondelete 'cascade' ; \r \r the  channelgroup_id  column actually refers to the group's dbid  and therefore the  dbid  column of  teamspeak_group  , not the id.\r \r the existing relation needs to be removed, and replaced with the correct one.
text: fix incorrect foreign key for channelgroup_id in teamspeak_channel_group_permission. the following foreign key is currently set for table  teamspeak_channel_group_permission :  $table->foreign 'channelgroup_id' ->references 'id' ->on 'teamspeak_group' ->ondelete 'cascade' ; \r \r the  channelgroup_id  column actually refers to the group's dbid  and therefore the  dbid  column of  teamspeak_group  , not the id.\r \r the existing relation needs to be removed, and replaced with the correct one.

title: possible race-condition in remoteservice
body: race-condition during ack checking is possible. for example, ack timeout = 1000 ms:\r \r 1. worker receives request in 999 ms sends ack, but it comes in 1010ms or something like that.\r 2. client checks ack in 1000 ms and throws remoteserviceacktimeoutexception\r 3. worker invokes method\r \r to solve this problem  ackobject  was introduced. worker or client set it to 1 via  setnx  command. worker set it during ack sending. client during ack receiving.\r \r client check ackobject only if ack timeout has occurred. if client can't set it means that worker have done it already. so client should poll an element from queue again.\r \r if worker can't set ackobject then it means that ack timeout already occurred on client side and invocation should skipped.

title: tools.reduce: matrix rank affecting the dimensions of the result
body: import hypertools as hyp \r  import numpy as np \r  print hyp.tools.reduce np.random.normal 0,1, 5,100  ,ndims=10 .shape \r \r this is the code i tried which was supposed to give me a 5x10 dimension matrix. however, because the rank of the matrix is 5, pca is unable to generate 10 dimensions from the data. therefore the resulting matrix i got was a 5x5 matrix.\r \r i talked about this issue with professor manning today, and he suggested a fix to this problem:\r if the number of dimensions to reduce to is greater than the rank of the matrix, then pad the matrix with rows of 0s to increase the rank, do pca then eliminate the 0 rows.\r \r could you look into this issue and let me know what you think? thanks!
text: tools.reduce: matrix rank affecting the dimensions of the result. import hypertools as hyp \r  import numpy as np \r  print hyp.tools.reduce np.random.normal 0,1, 5,100  ,ndims=10 .shape \r \r this is the code i tried which was supposed to give me a 5x10 dimension matrix. however, because the rank of the matrix is 5, pca is unable to generate 10 dimensions from the data. therefore the resulting matrix i got was a 5x5 matrix.\r \r i talked about this issue with professor manning today, and he suggested a fix to this problem:\r if the number of dimensions to reduce to is greater than the rank of the matrix, then pad the matrix with rows of 0s to increase the rank, do pca then eliminate the 0 rows.\r \r could you look into this issue and let me know what you think? thanks!

title: declined transactions are not caught
body: longclaw version: 0.2.0\r   django version: 1.10\r   python version: 3.5\r \r     description\r transactions which are declined by the payment gateway  in this case braintree - unknown if it occurs with all gateways  still appear as orders in the longclaw admin:\r \r ! image  https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2784823/32183109-4fec0b68-bd90-11e7-8da8-bc43482bce48.png \r \r the braintree admin however, indicates that this transaction was declined by the customers bank account due to insufficient funds. this is apparently not the same as a rejection:\r \r https://articles.braintreepayments.com/control-panel/transactions/declines\r \r it is more than likely that more advanced handling of different potential transaction states & statuses is required\r

title: can only respawn in current dimension in a bed.  v1.0.2 mc1.10.2 forge-12.18.3.2254
body: if choose a respawn point in a dimension other than the one i died in i respawn at the correct co-ords, but i am not properly teleported into the dimension i chose. instead, i stay in the dimension i died in. this often results in falling out of the sky or suffocation.\r \r additionally, choosing the world spawn option results in spawning close to the correct location, only underground!\r \r choosing a bed in the dimension of death works correctly.\r \r i really hope there is a simple fix as this was going to be an important part of my modpack. this issue persists even after a clean install with only this mod.

title: comments within a nsfw thread incorrectly assigned sfw status
body: reddit's api doesn't report a comment within an nsfw thread to be over18. however, if you use the actual reddit.com/u/user/saved you will see that such posts are marked as nsfw. that, i would say, is the expected behavior.\r \r a possible solution is to actually check on the reddit api for each subreddit to see if a subreddit is nsfw, and mark all items belonging to that subreddit as nsfw. that would entail, once all saved posts are retrieved ant the subreddits lists generated, map over that list to generate a cmd to request data from the reddit api for each subreddit, and then update all the info. as this would entail possibly generating a lot of request, a  cmd batcher  https://github.com/simplynaoh/dailytoggl/blob/master/src/cmddispatcher.elm  might be needed.\r \r another possibility is, for comments, asking the reddit api for the parent information. if the parent is nsfw, then so should be the comment.\r

body: _this issue was originally opened by @wishcow as hashicorp/terraform 8733. it was migrated here as part of the  provider split  https://www.hashicorp.com/blog/upcoming-provider-changes-in-terraform-0-10/ . the original body of the issue is below._\r \r <hr>\r \r     terraform version  terraform v0.7.3     affected resource s  - aws_vpc - aws_vpc_peering_connection     terraform configuration files      hcl variable \ kops_cidr_block\  {} variable \ tf_cidr_block\  {} variable \ peer_owner_id\  {}  resource \ aws_vpc\  \ kops\  {     cidr_block = \ ${var.kops_cidr_block}\      enable_dns_hostnames = true     tags {         name = \ kops vpc\          managed = \ terraform\      } }  resource \ aws_vpc\  \ tf\  {     cidr_block = \ ${var.tf_cidr_block}\      enable_dns_hostnames = true     tags {         name = \ terraform vpc\          managed = \ terraform\      } }  resource \ aws_vpc_peering_connection\  \ kops-tf\  {     peer_owner_id = \ ${var.peer_owner_id}\      peer_vpc_id = \ ${aws_vpc.tf.id}\      vpc_id = \ ${aws_vpc.kops.id}\      auto_accept = true      accepter {         allow_remote_vpc_dns_resolution = true     }     requester {         allow_remote_vpc_dns_resolution = true     }      tags {         managed = \ terraform\          name = \ kops-tf\      } }          debug output  the debug output seems to contain some sensitive information, i'm not sure i'm allowed to share that  company policies .     panic output  no panic output     expected behavior  expected the vpc peering connection to be made, with remote_vpc_dns_resolution enabled.     actual behavior  the vpc peering connection is made, but the dns resolution properties are disabled. in addition, terraform outputs the following error:  error applying plan:  1 error s  occurred: - title: tox envlist selects incorrect python versions
body: minimal reproducible example and detailed description\r if a python version is not available for a defined environment in the  envlist  tox selects the default python for some environments and fails with  interpreternotfound  for others.  this should be consistent and fail for all versions of python that aren't available.\r \r     prerequisites:\r - must have at least one version of python installed  python 3.7.0 in this example \r - must __not__ have at least one of the  affected  python environments installed\r - must __not__ have at least one of the  unaffected  python environments installed\r - must have  tox  installed in the current environment\r \r     reproduce:\r      create the following  tox.ini  in the current directory:\r    ini\r  tox \r envlist =\r ;    affected python environments  not comprehensive \r ;    the following will not fail if they are unavailable\r     py25\r     py33\r     py40\r ;    unaffected python environments  not comprehensive \r ;    the expected \ interpreternotfound\  error will be displayed if they are unavailable\r     py34\r     py35\r \r skipsdist = true\r \r  testenv \r commands =\r     python --version\r    \r      check current python version\r in this example python 3.7.0 is the system default, your version may differ\r    \r >python --version\r python 3.7.0\r    \r      run test and review output\r only one success and one failure shown for brevity:\r    shell\r >python -m tox\r python -m tox\r py25 create: e:\\src\\toxin\\.tox\\py25\r py25 runtests: pythonhashseed='50'\r py25 runtests: commands 0  | python --version\r python 3.7.0\r ...\r py34 create: e:\\src\\toxin\\.tox\\py34\r error: interpreternotfound: python3.4\r    \r python 2.5 is not installed but an environment for python 2.5 was created   py25 create: e:\\src\\toxin\\.tox\\py25   but is using the current system default python 3.7.0\r \r python 3.4 is not installed and the expected error is displayed   error: interpreternotfound: python3.4  \r \r   os and  pip list  output\r    shell\r >python -c \ import platform; print platform.platform   \ \r windows-10-10.0.17134-sp0\r \r >py -0\r installed pythons found by py launcher for windows\r  -3.7-32  \r  -3.6-32\r  -2.7-32\r \r >pip list\r packaging  17.1 \r pip  9.0.1 \r pluggy  0.6.0 \r py  1.5.4 \r pyparsing  2.2.0 \r setuptools  28.8.0 \r six  1.11.0 \r tox  3.1.0 \r virtualenv  16.0.0 \r    \r

title: \ no transaction in context\  after context refresh with spring-boot-devtools
body: i'm getting the following error when spring context is refreshed with  spring-boot-devtools :\r    \r java.lang.illegalstateexception: no transaction in context.\r \tat org.jetbrains.exposed.sql.transactions.transactionmanager$companion.current transactionapi.kt:70  ~ exposed-0.10.4.jar:na \r \tat org.jetbrains.exposed.sql.vendors.defaultkt.getcurrentdialect default.kt:339  ~ exposed-0.10.4.jar:na \r \tat org.jetbrains.exposed.sql.schemautils.drop schemautils.kt:189  ~ exposed-0.10.4.jar:na \r \tat com.alexbezhan.exposeddemo.init.init exposeddemoapplication.kt:43  ~ classes/:na \r    \r \r the issue occurs when i provide exposed table as a spring bean like this:\r    \r interface repository\r object table : intidtable  , repository\r \r // and then in configuration:\r @bean\r fun repo  : repository = table\r    \r \r and then i drop the table with  schemautils  on  contextrefreshedevent :\r    \r schemautils.drop table \r    \r \r here is the minimal code to reproduce:\r    \r @enabletransactionmanagement\r @springbootapplication\r class exposeddemoapplication {\r     // this causes error after context refresh with spring devtools\r     @bean\r     fun repo  : repository = table\r \r     @bean\r     fun springtransactionmanager datasource: datasource : springtransactionmanager =\r             springtransactionmanager datasource \r }\r \r interface repository\r \r object table : intidtable  , repository\r \r @transactional\r @component\r class init {\r     @eventlistener\r     fun init event: contextrefreshedevent  {\r         println \ init  \  \r         schemautils.drop table \r         schemautils.create table \r     }\r \r }\r \r fun main args: array<string>  {\r     runapplication<exposeddemoapplication>  args \r }\r    \r if i don't provide table as a bean, then no exception is thrown.\r \r i also created  demo project  https://github.com/alexbezhan/exposed-spring-devtools-issue  that reproduces the issue.

title: problem with @builder and generics
body: have a look at this code:\r    \r @getter\r @allargsconstructor\r class foofoo {\r \tprivate string text;\r }\r \r @getter\r class barbar<t> extends foofoo {\r \tprivate t content;\r \r \t@builder\r \tpublic barbar string text, t content  {\r \t\tsuper text ;\r \t\tthis.content = content;\r \t}\r }\r \r class main {\r \tpublic static void main string   args  {\r \t\tbarbar<bigdecimal> barbar = barbar.<bigdecimal>builder  .text \ hiho\  .content bigdecimal.one .build  ;\r \t}\r }\r    \r \r for some reasons my ide  intellij idea  complains about  \ .content bigdecimal.one \  with \ content t  cannot be applied to content bigdecimal \ .\r however the code compiles fine!\r \r when i use the delombok feature of the plugin, it creates a non-generic builder and the delomboked code does not compile at all.\r i need to add <t> parameter to the generated builder to make it work.
text: problem with @builder and generics. have a look at this code:\r    \r @getter\r @allargsconstructor\r class foofoo {\r \tprivate string text;\r }\r \r @getter\r class barbar<t> extends foofoo {\r \tprivate t content;\r \r \t@builder\r \tpublic barbar string text, t content  {\r \t\tsuper text ;\r \t\tthis.content = content;\r \t}\r }\r \r class main {\r \tpublic static void main string   args  {\r \t\tbarbar<bigdecimal> barbar = barbar.<bigdecimal>builder  .text \ hiho\  .content bigdecimal.one .build  ;\r \t}\r }\r    \r \r for some reasons my ide  intellij idea  complains about  \ .content bigdecimal.one \  with \ content t  cannot be applied to content bigdecimal \ .\r however the code compiles fine!\r \r when i use the delombok feature of the plugin, it creates a non-generic builder and the delomboked code does not compile at all.\r i need to add <t> parameter to the generated builder to make it work.

title: last step in show loop is only shown for very short time
body: if this show loops  with loop: -1  it will only show the last step for one step  2ms at 50hz . this is because we set time to 0 for the first step  why?  and when it loops it does not wait after the last step.\r      rainbow2:\r       - leds:\r            leds : aliceblue\r       - leds:\r            leds : antiquewhite\r       - leds:\r            leds : aquamarine\r       - leds:\r            leds : azure

title: sqlite strategy needs to await creating tables
body: in the sqlite strategy we create the necessary table in the constructor, but we  don't await on the promise.  https://github.com/shopify/shopify-express/blob/master/shopstore/sqlitestrategy.js l13 . \r \r this is potentially a problem since the action might not be done until significantly after the constructor finishes if the file system is slow for some reason.
text: sqlite strategy needs to await creating tables. in the sqlite strategy we create the necessary table in the constructor, but we  don't await on the promise.  https://github.com/shopify/shopify-express/blob/master/shopstore/sqlitestrategy.js l13 . \r \r this is potentially a problem since the action might not be done until significantly after the constructor finishes if the file system is slow for some reason.

title: race condition on using back/forward to change url
body: because the sagas listening for state changes to update the url run asynchronously, they tend to run just as the saga listening for url changes is running.  this then causes a back-and-forth update remove of url based on state and locks up the browser.\r \r the solution is to put an action marking the beginning of state transitions from a url, and then another action marking the end, much like begin/commit from sql world.  then the locationfromstate listener will simply pause on begin until commit is received.
text: race condition on using back/forward to change url. because the sagas listening for state changes to update the url run asynchronously, they tend to run just as the saga listening for url changes is running.  this then causes a back-and-forth update remove of url based on state and locks up the browser.\r \r the solution is to put an action marking the beginning of state transitions from a url, and then another action marking the end, much like begin/commit from sql world.  then the locationfromstate listener will simply pause on begin until commit is received.

title: downstream fedora patch: prevent check_swap from returning ok, if no swap  activated
body: this is an old patch we have been putting into nagios for a while. i am putting it in to get it looked and commented on by upstream.\r \r from: jan ondrej <ondrejj@salstar.sk>\r date: thu, 7 oct 2010 17:28:48 +0400\r subject:  patch 05/10  prevent check_swap from returning ok, if no swap\r  activated\r \r my swap was not activated on boot for unknown reason and nagios does not\r report this as a problem. here is an example:\r \r  root@kecom ~   rpm -q nagios-plugins\r nagios-plugins-1.4.13-11.fc10.i386\r  root@kecom ~   /usr/lib/nagios/plugins/check_swap -w 80% -c 40% -c 1 -w 2\r swap critical - 100% free  0 mb out of 0 mb  |swap=0mb;0;0;0;0\r \r if there is no swap and users is trying to test percentage of free swap,\r consider 0 mb free swap space as problem, or of free/total raises division\r by zero, then set percentage to 0%, not to 100%.\r \r steps to reproduce:\r 1. make sure, your swap is empty or it's usage is not large\r 2. swapoff -a\r 3. /usr/lib/nagios/plugins/check_swap -w 80% -c 40%\r \r actual results:\r swap ok - 100% free  0 mb out of 0 mb  |swap=0mb;0;0;0;0\r \r expected results:\r swap critical - 0% free  0 mb out of 0 mb  |swap=0mb;0;0;0;0\r \r additional info:\r https://bugzilla.redhat.com/512559\r    \r  plugins/check_swap.c | 2 +-\r  1 file changed, 1 insertion + , 1 deletion - \r \r diff --git a/plugins/check_swap.c b/plugins/check_swap.c\r index ce1f602..6d1d0c1 100644\r --- a/plugins/check_swap.c\r +++ b/plugins/check_swap.c\r @@ -124,7 +124,7 @@ main  int argc, char   argv \r                         free_swap_mb += dskfree_mb;\r                         if  allswaps  {\r                                 if  dsktotal_mb == 0 \r -                                       percent=100.0;\r +                                       percent = 0.0;\r                                 else\r                                         percent = 100      double  dskused_mb  /   double  dsktotal_mb  ;\r                                 result = max_state  result, check_swap  percent, dskfree_mb  ;\r -- \r 1.8.3.1\r

title: enemies keep chasing even on map switch
body: when the player gets ambushed by the 3 dogfish and then tries to run away to the previous map, the 3 dogfish will be there on different tiles.\r i guess this was not intended and monsters shouldn't be transferable between maps, similar to nethack's behaviour.\r \r in addition, for some reason the sound of dogfish snarl is being accompanied by the water pour over the fire as happened in the first map.\r \r so, these are the two bug fixes we should address:\r \r -      restrict npcs to their native map such that they shouldn't be able to move between maps.\r -  x   seperate between dogfish snarl and the water sound on river ambush.
text: enemies keep chasing even on map switch. when the player gets ambushed by the 3 dogfish and then tries to run away to the previous map, the 3 dogfish will be there on different tiles.\r i guess this was not intended and monsters shouldn't be transferable between maps, similar to nethack's behaviour.\r \r in addition, for some reason the sound of dogfish snarl is being accompanied by the water pour over the fire as happened in the first map.\r \r so, these are the two bug fixes we should address:\r \r -      restrict npcs to their native map such that they shouldn't be able to move between maps.\r -  x   seperate between dogfish snarl and the water sound on river ambush.

title: superblock header is formatted after segment headers are formatted
body: trivial bug.\r \r in the following code, format_superblock_header and format_all_segment_headers should be swapped because if the former succeeds but the latter failed, the next dmsetup create can resume the cache but the segment headers are broken.\r \r    c\r static int format_cache_device struct wb_device  wb \r {\r \tint r = 0;\r \tstruct dm_dev  dev = wb->cache_dev;\r \r \tr = zeroing_full_superblock wb ;\r \tif  r  {\r \t\tdmerr \ zeroing_full_superblock failed\  ;\r \t\treturn r;\r \t}\r \tr = format_superblock_header wb ; /  first 512b  /\r \tif  r  {\r \t\tdmerr \ format_superblock_header failed\  ;\r \t\treturn r;\r \t}\r \tr = format_all_segment_headers wb ;\r \tif  r  {\r \t\tdmerr \ format_all_segment_headers failed\  ;\r \t\treturn r;\r \t}\r \tr = blkdev_issue_flush dev->bdev, gfp_kernel, null ;\r \r \treturn r;\r }\r
text: superblock header is formatted after segment headers are formatted. trivial bug.\r \r in the following code, format_superblock_header and format_all_segment_headers should be swapped because if the former succeeds but the latter failed, the next dmsetup create can resume the cache but the segment headers are broken.\r \r    c\r static int format_cache_device struct wb_device  wb \r {\r \tint r = 0;\r \tstruct dm_dev  dev = wb->cache_dev;\r \r \tr = zeroing_full_superblock wb ;\r \tif  r  {\r \t\tdmerr \ zeroing_full_superblock failed\  ;\r \t\treturn r;\r \t}\r \tr = format_superblock_header wb ; /  first 512b  /\r \tif  r  {\r \t\tdmerr \ format_superblock_header failed\  ;\r \t\treturn r;\r \t}\r \tr = format_all_segment_headers wb ;\r \tif  r  {\r \t\tdmerr \ format_all_segment_headers failed\  ;\r \t\treturn r;\r \t}\r \tr = blkdev_issue_flush dev->bdev, gfp_kernel, null ;\r \r \treturn r;\r }\r

title: cli: /data/obs fetch of expression data is very slow on immune_bone_marrow dataset
body: a simple fetch of a expression data via the rest api is very slow on the immune_bone_marrow dataset.   the bottleneck seems to be in the cli processing - it is definitely not in the data transfer.  if i make a request twice, it takes 90 seconds to fetch the result.  second attempt takes 2 seconds  essentially the data transfer time .  given the trivial nature of the data filtering  eg, return an entire column for the dataframe of 378k rows , this seems extremely slow.        curl -x put http://localhost:5005/api/v0.2/data/obs -h \ content-type: application/json\  -d '{\ filter\ :{\ var\ :{\ annotation_value\ : {\ name\ :\ gene_names\ ,\ values\ : \ rp11-34p13.7\  } }}}' >  /dev/null   % total    % received % xferd  average speed   time    time     time  current                                  dload  upload   total   spent    left  speed 100 14.6m  100 14.6m  100    89   290k      1  0:01:29  0:00:51  0:00:38 3496k root@woof:/mnt/c/users/bruce/projects/cellxgene/client  curl -x put http://localhost:5005/api/v0.2/data/obs -h \ content-type: application/json\  -d '{\ filter\ :{\ var\ :{\ annotation_value\ : {\ name\ :\ gene_names\ ,\ values\ : \ rp11-34p13.7\  } }}}' > /dev/null   % total    % received % xferd  average speed   time    time     time  current                                  dload  upload   total   spent    left  speed 100 14.6m  100 14.6m  100    89  6919k     40  0:00:02  0:00:02 --:--:-- 6922k      i get a very similar result when using the get interface, eg.,  /data/obs?var:name=3   digging into the cli code a bit, it looks like almost all of the time is spent in  scanpy_engine.py::filter_dataframe   , in these two lines  near bottom of function :              cell_data = self.data cells_idx, :          data = cell_data :, genes_idx       i'm not sure if this is due to the way we are slicing, or the nature of the data, but it causes quite a noticeable delay in the ui.  @freeman-lab - the above rest request is simply looking for the observation-major data, for all observations, for the column where the annotation gene_names=='rp11-34p1.7'.

title: hdfs assetstore may prematurely delete duplicated files
body: the hdfs assetstore adapter does not check the reference count for underlying data before deleting it. if someone copied a file stored in an hdfs assetstore and deleted one of the copies, it would invalidate the other copy.
text: hdfs assetstore may prematurely delete duplicated files. the hdfs assetstore adapter does not check the reference count for underlying data before deleting it. if someone copied a file stored in an hdfs assetstore and deleted one of the copies, it would invalidate the other copy.

title: calicoctl node status sometimes doesn't show the bgp sessions
body: hi,\r \r i'm testing the v1.0.0-beta of calicoctl, and after setting up a bgp session with my router, the commando 'calicoctl node status' sometimes doesn't show the bgp session.\r \r output as can be seen:\r \r    \r  root@local-mesos-slave-001 ~   calicoctl node status\r calico process is running.\r \r ipv4 bgp status\r +---------------+-----------+-------+----------+-------------+\r | peer address  | peer type | state |  since   |    info     |\r +---------------+-----------+-------+----------+-------------+\r | 192.168.56.15 | global    | up    | 12:58:40 | established |\r +---------------+-----------+-------+----------+-------------+\r \r ipv6 bgp status\r no ipv6 peers found.\r \r  root@local-mesos-slave-001 ~   calicoctl node status\r calico process is running.\r \r ipv4 bgp status\r no ipv4 peers found.\r \r ipv6 bgp status\r no ipv6 peers found.\r    \r \r my setup is as follows:\r - 1 mesos-master\r - 2 mesos-agents\r - 1 router\r \r i'm running this within vagrant / virtualbox on my laptop, based on rhel7.\r \r i disabled the node-to-node bgp mesh option, and configured the bgp session with the router with this yaml file:\r \r    \r - apiversion: v1\r   kind: bgppeer\r   metadata:\r     scope: global\r     peerip: 192.168.56.15\r   spec:\r     asnumber: 64520\r    \r \r attached is the output of  calico node diags .\r  diags-20161107_142526.tar.gz  https://github.com/projectcalico/calico-containers/files/575433/diags-20161107_142526.tar.gz \r

title: cvc4 in pysmt.

title: \ command+plus\  accelerator appears in menu as \ shift command =\  instead of \ command +\  or \ shift command +\  on os x
body: electron versions: at least 0.23.0 and 0.24.0\r   os: mac\r \r the  list of key codes  https://github.com/atom/electron/blob/master/docs/api/accelerator.md available-key-codes  available for use in menu item accelerators includes  plus .  when this key is used in an accelerator like  command+plus  on os x, the key combination appears as \ shift command =\ .  given that the key name is  plus , it would be preferable to have the corresponding key combination appear as \ +\  or \ shift +\  instead of \ shift =\ .  the  keyboard shortcut section of the os x human interface guidelines  https://developer.apple.com/library/mac/documentation/userexperience/conceptual/osxhiguidelines/keyboard.html  indicates that \ shift command +\  is preferable to \ shift command =\ --look for the paragraph starting with \ identify a key with two characters.\ \r \r a minimal electron app that demonstrates this behavior can be found  in this gist  https://gist.github.com/mark-wagon/e4765596cbd9580e26a9 .  repro steps are outlined in the gist's readme.
text: \ command+plus\  accelerator appears in menu as \ shift command =\  instead of \ command +\  or \ shift command +\  on os x. electron versions: at least 0.23.0 and 0.24.0\r   os: mac\r \r the  list of key codes  https://github.com/atom/electron/blob/master/docs/api/accelerator.md available-key-codes  available for use in menu item accelerators includes  plus .  when this key is used in an accelerator like  command+plus  on os x, the key combination appears as \ shift command =\ .  given that the key name is  plus , it would be preferable to have the corresponding key combination appear as \ +\  or \ shift +\  instead of \ shift =\ .  the  keyboard shortcut section of the os x human interface guidelines  https://developer.apple.com/library/mac/documentation/userexperience/conceptual/osxhiguidelines/keyboard.html  indicates that \ shift command +\  is preferable to \ shift command =\ --look for the paragraph starting with \ identify a key with two characters.\ \r \r a minimal electron app that demonstrates this behavior can be found  in this gist  https://gist.github.com/mark-wagon/e4765596cbd9580e26a9 .  repro steps are outlined in the gist's readme.

title: esc keybinding overrides the material-ui keybindings for the \ delete notebook\  dialog
body: this was surfaced by  280. i cannot escape the delete notebook confirmation dialog because we've overridden the keybinding. this ui issue is breaking convention so imo this is a rough edge at best, bug at worst.\r \r a dialog should require a notebook mode state change, i'm thinking, so we can get out of all the notebook-specific keybindings by not being in \ command\  or \ edit\ .\r \r we already do this for updating the title. i would argue we should simply generalize this and apply it to any moment we want to completely escape the notebook keybindings.
text: esc keybinding overrides the material-ui keybindings for the \ delete notebook\  dialog. this was surfaced by  280. i cannot escape the delete notebook confirmation dialog because we've overridden the keybinding. this ui issue is breaking convention so imo this is a rough edge at best, bug at worst.\r \r a dialog should require a notebook mode state change, i'm thinking, so we can get out of all the notebook-specific keybindings by not being in \ command\  or \ edit\ .\r \r we already do this for updating the title. i would argue we should simply generalize this and apply it to any moment we want to completely escape the notebook keybindings.

title: signup for deactivated realms throws an error after confirmation
body: if you deactivate a realm using e.g.  ./manage.py deactivate_realm zulip.com  and then go through the registration flow, it currently doesn't give you an error about the realm having been deactivated until after you click the email confirmation link.  it seems like we could reject you earlier.

title: api  next  json key loops on itself with max_page_size=0 and limit=0
body: environment\r   python version:  3.7\r   netbox version:  2.4.4\r \r     steps to reproduce\r \r set  max_page_size=0  to disable the pagination, and do a get on a list of items   /api/dcim/devices/  for example  with the get parameter  limit=0 .\r \r <!-- what did you expect to happen? -->\r     expected behavior\r \r the  next  value in the response should be  null , as the pagination is disabled.\r \r <!-- what happened instead? -->\r     observed behavior\r \r the  next  is looping on the same get query, with  limit=0&offset=0    /api/dcim/devices/?limit=0&offset=0  for example . it is an issue for some api clients that just loop over the  next  key until it is null.
text: api  next  json key loops on itself with max_page_size=0 and limit=0. environment\r   python version:  3.7\r   netbox version:  2.4.4\r \r     steps to reproduce\r \r set  max_page_size=0  to disable the pagination, and do a get on a list of items   /api/dcim/devices/  for example  with the get parameter  limit=0 .\r \r <!-- what did you expect to happen? -->\r     expected behavior\r \r the  next  value in the response should be  null , as the pagination is disabled.\r \r <!-- what happened instead? -->\r     observed behavior\r \r the  next  is looping on the same get query, with  limit=0&offset=0    /api/dcim/devices/?limit=0&offset=0  for example . it is an issue for some api clients that just loop over the  next  key until it is null.

title: novnc get stuck when connect to qemu on a low speed network
body: if using novnc to connect to a qemu instance on a low speed network  e.g. 300kb/s wide area network , the novnc will be stuck when it receives a lot of frame buffer update.\r \r the fastest way to reproduce:\r \r 1. run a qemu instance with option -vnc :1,websocket\r 2. connect novnc client to qemu vm via websocket\r 3. play a video on vm  to generates a lot of fbus \r 4. limit the client inbound bandwidth to 300kb/s  to simulate a low speed wan \r 5. then novnc get stuck, the cursor is almost impossible to move\r \r i know that novnc is not designed for multimedia but actually it works well with sufficient bandwidth  e.g. local area network .\r \r i tried using vncviewer to connect to qemu in the same environment, although a lot of frame loss, it still works.\r \r by contrast, i found that novnc   never drop any frame  , it tried to display every frame\r even when the network is congested so it will stuck.\r \r as far as i'm concerned, it looks like a bug between qemu and novnc. \r \r information about the environment:  tested version \r qemu 2.7.1/2.9.0/2.10.0\r host os: ubuntu 16.04 server lts\r guest vm  os: windows 7 64bit\r client os: windows 7 64bit\r client browser: ie 11.0.9600/chrome 60.0.3112\r novnc: 0.51/0.61/0.62\r \r any help much appreciated.

title: uialertview causes nsinvalidargumentexception  incompatible signatures
body: i'm experiencing an intermittent bug when showing uialertview's through blockskit's shortcut  bk_showalertviewwithtitle:... .  the crash:\r \r         terminating app due to uncaught exception 'nsinvalidargumentexception', reason: 'attempted to create block invocation with incompatible signatures'\r \r if you comment out the lines 177-179 in a2blockinvocation.m, then everything works fine.  it seems to be more prevalent in older generation phones  iphone 4/4s, iphone 5... .  could this have something to do with how  issignature:compatiblewithsignature:  works on 32 bit devices vs 64 bit devices?\r \r as for whether or not the block signatures are actually incorrect... i've triple checked every single one and they're correct.

title: usability issues with --changed-parent=head
body: in some cases i'd like to be able to make a bunch of changes in my repo, then compile just the targets that are changed.  i've been playing with --changed-parent=head and while it works in some cases, it gives errors in other cases that prevent it from being effective.\r \r for example, if i create a new file in a new directory  with no build file , i get an error:\r \r     mkdir bin\r     echo \ hi\  > bin/readme.txt\r     ./pants --changed-parent=head list \r     exception caught:  <class 'pants.engine.mapper.resolveerror'> \r \r     exception message: path \ bin\  contains no build files.\r \r if i delete a directory with a build file, i also get an error that the build file doesn't exist  it doesn't anymore .\r \r if i change a source file that is referenced by an existing build file, it works as expected  listing/compiling the target affected .\r \r i would expect if i delete a directory/file, or modify/add a file somewhere that's not referenced by a pants build, pants would just ignore these changes and list/compile any remaining targets that are modified.\r \r \r

body: using this docker-compose file\r    yaml\r version: '2' \r services:\r   foo:\r     image: foo \r \r   bar:\r     extends: foo\r    \r \r libcompose fails as:\r    bash\r $ libcompose up\r warn 0000  note: this is an experimental alternate implementation of the compose cli  https://github.com/docker/compose  \r erro 0000  could not parse config for project libcom : service 'bar' has neither an image nor a build context specified. at least one must be provided. \r fata 0000  failed to read project: service 'bar' has neither an image nor a build context specified. at least one must be provided. \r    \r \r when using extends in following format, it works fine:\r \r    yaml\r version: '2' \r services:\r   foo:\r     image: foo \r \r   bar:\r     extends:\r       service: foo\r    \r \r libcompose worked fine:\r    bash\r $ libcompose up\r warn 0000  note: this is an experimental alternate implementation of the compose cli  https://github.com/docker/compose  \r creating network \ libcom_default\  with driver \ \ \r info 0000   0/2   foo : starting                        \r info 0000   0/2   bar : starting                        \r pulling foo  foo ...\r <snip>\r    \r \r ref: https://github.com/kubernetes-incubator/kompose/issues/357
