title: first time build failing
body: the builds on travis are failing because of an issue where you begin with a clean checkout  ensure the  database  folder does not exist . run  mvn clean package  and the build will fail because the h2 database has not been created.\r \r as it fails, it will create the database, so subsequent runs will succeed. unfortunately as travis uses a new worker for every job  good practice , the build will never pass.
text: first time build failing. the builds on travis are failing because of an issue where you begin with a clean checkout  ensure the  database  folder does not exist . run  mvn clean package  and the build will fail because the h2 database has not been created.\r \r as it fails, it will create the database, so subsequent runs will succeed. unfortunately as travis uses a new worker for every job  good practice , the build will never pass.

title: improve logging of helm policy provider
body: enabling the helm provider triggers an  error  level log entry each second for each installed helm chart without a keel policy.\r \r    \r level=error msg=\ provider.helm: failed to get config for release\  error=\ policy not specified\  \r    \r \r specifying a wrong string is an  info  level message without decent traceback:\r    \r level=info msg=\ unknown policy 'dont'\ \r    \r \r i'd love it if keel would either ignore helm charts without policies  which is kinda consistent with the annotation behaviour , or allow me to specify a kind of 'ignore this' policy.\r
text: improve logging of helm policy provider. enabling the helm provider triggers an  error  level log entry each second for each installed helm chart without a keel policy.\r \r    \r level=error msg=\ provider.helm: failed to get config for release\  error=\ policy not specified\  \r    \r \r specifying a wrong string is an  info  level message without decent traceback:\r    \r level=info msg=\ unknown policy 'dont'\ \r    \r \r i'd love it if keel would either ignore helm charts without policies  which is kinda consistent with the annotation behaviour , or allow me to specify a kind of 'ignore this' policy.\r

title: bug on result rootrect
body: hi\r \r it seems like there is a bug on the calculation of the rootrect returned by the quadtree. it's visible if you use less pins on the map the click on a cluster. it will zoom on the rect defined by the first pin not the rect defined by the group of pins. i don't know if i'm clear tho. i will investigate more and try to provide you a simple way to detect it.
text: bug on result rootrect. hi\r \r it seems like there is a bug on the calculation of the rootrect returned by the quadtree. it's visible if you use less pins on the map the click on a cluster. it will zoom on the rect defined by the first pin not the rect defined by the group of pins. i don't know if i'm clear tho. i will investigate more and try to provide you a simple way to detect it.

title: plugin doesn't seem to support -r in requirements.txt
body: i have a  requirements.txt  file like this:\r    \r -r requirements-pipenv.txt\r -e ./api\r    \r because  pipenv install  doesn't work with  -e .  you could also imagine different uses for  -r  in this file, such as  those discussed here  http://www.revsys.com/blog/2014/nov/21/recommended-django-project-layout/ . \r \r when i  sis deploy , though, i get this message:\r    \r could not open requirements file:  errno 2  no such file or directory: '.serverless/requirements-pipenv.txt'\r    \r this appears to be because this plugin only copies  requirements.txt  and not files that it includes. i looked at the readme and didn't see a way to make it include other files, but i might have missed something.\r \r it would be useful if the plugin either:\r 1. auto-discovered  -r  lines in  requirements.txt  and added those files as well; and/or\r 2. allowed me to explicitly configure what files to include

title: can't be used on the rhs of  letstx
body: js\r macro test {\r   case { _ } => {\r     letstx $foo =  { 1 + 2 + 3 };\r     return  {\r       $foo;\r     }\r   }\r }\r test\r    \r \r this is because     breaks hygiene, referring to  name_stx  and  match , and  letstx  defers to helper macros, so it gets the wrong  name_stx  and  match  when calling  transcibe . also illustrated by http://bit.ly/1fmruwf\r \r to clarify, this is a problem with      well, really all fundamental macros that break hygiene , i just titled the issue the way i did because it's how you'd likely happen upon it.

title: remove \ session\  from \ set transaction\  statement
body: the  begintransaction  method executes a  set session transaction isolation level  statement   here  https://github.com/mysql-net/mysqlconnector/blob/master/src/mysqlconnector/mysqlclient/mysqlconnection.cs l72  .\r \r as per  14.3.6 set transaction syntax  https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.7/en/set-transaction.html :\r \r >   with the  session  keyword, the statement applies to all subsequent transactions performed within the current session.\r >   without any  session  or  global  keyword, the statement applies to the next  not started  transaction performed within the current session. subsequent transactions revert to using the  session  isolation level.\r \r the use of the  session  keyword is inappropriate here, as we only want to change the isolation level for the immediate next transaction.\r \r  see also  mysql bug 86263  https://bugs.mysql.com/bug.php?id=86263 .
text: remove \ session\  from \ set transaction\  statement. the  begintransaction  method executes a  set session transaction isolation level  statement   here  https://github.com/mysql-net/mysqlconnector/blob/master/src/mysqlconnector/mysqlclient/mysqlconnection.cs l72  .\r \r as per  14.3.6 set transaction syntax  https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.7/en/set-transaction.html :\r \r >   with the  session  keyword, the statement applies to all subsequent transactions performed within the current session.\r >   without any  session  or  global  keyword, the statement applies to the next  not started  transaction performed within the current session. subsequent transactions revert to using the  session  isolation level.\r \r the use of the  session  keyword is inappropriate here, as we only want to change the isolation level for the immediate next transaction.\r \r  see also  mysql bug 86263  https://bugs.mysql.com/bug.php?id=86263 .

title: idps loading additional metadata using filebackedhttpmetadataprovider fail
body: if an idp were to load some third party metadata using the filebackedhttpmetadataprovider method and attempt to have the metadata stored in  {{ shib_idp.home }}/metadata then the metadata load will fail due to file permissions. the directory proctections do not allow a jetty process to write to the directory.
text: idps loading additional metadata using filebackedhttpmetadataprovider fail. if an idp were to load some third party metadata using the filebackedhttpmetadataprovider method and attempt to have the metadata stored in  {{ shib_idp.home }}/metadata then the metadata load will fail due to file permissions. the directory proctections do not allow a jetty process to write to the directory.

title: the left editor nav doesn't respond to the table resize
body: when i drag the table bottom to resize it, the left nav doesn't resize itself.\r \r \r
